# Runtime Manager - Full Documentation

# Payload Formats and Logging

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

To send message payload data to external analytics software, Runtime Manager agent must first
serialize and convert the data to text.

Because some payload formats canât be converted to plain text, Runtime Manager agent converts only
those formats that are considered to work. When the payload format canât be converted, the agent
passes only the fully qualified name of the payload class type, not the payload message content.

> [!NOTE] Runtime Manager agent version 1.6.2 and earlier attempts to convert all payload formats,
> which results in failures for some formats.

## Default Conversion Behavior

The following table lists the payload formats and specifies whether Runtime Manager agent converts
them to the String type and passes the payload message. If the **Converted to String** column shows
FALSE, Runtime Manager agent passes only the payload class type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Extension</th><th>Kind of document</th><th>MIME Type</th><th>Converted to String</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>.epub</p></td><td><p>Electronic publication (EPUB)</p></td><td><p>application/epub+zip</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.jar</p></td><td><p>Java Archive (JAR)</p></td><td><p>application/java-archive</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.js</p></td><td><p>JavaScript (ECMAScript)</p></td><td><p>application/javascript</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.json</p></td><td><p>JSON format</p></td><td><p>application/json</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.doc</p></td><td><p>Microsoft Word</p></td><td><p>application/msword</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.arc</p></td><td><p>Archive document (multiple files embedded)</p></td><td><p>application/octet-stream</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.bin</p></td><td><p>Any kind of binary data</p></td><td><p>application/octet-stream</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ogx</p></td><td><p>OGG</p></td><td><p>application/ogg</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.pdf</p></td><td><p>Adobe Portable Document Format (PDF)</p></td><td><p>application/pdf</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.rtf</p></td><td><p>Rich Text Format (RTF)</p></td><td><p>application/rtf</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.azw</p></td><td><p>Amazon Kindle eBook format</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.amazon.ebook</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.mpkg</p></td><td><p>Apple Installer Package</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.apple.installer+xml</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.xul</p></td><td><p>XUL</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.mozilla.xul+xml</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.xls</p></td><td><p>Microsoft Excel</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.ms-excel</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ppt</p></td><td><p>Microsoft PowerPoint</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.ms-powerpoint</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.odp</p></td><td><p>OpenDocument presentation document</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.presentation</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ods</p></td><td><p>OpenDocument spreadsheet document</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.spreadsheet</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.odt</p></td><td><p>OpenDocument text document</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.vsd</p></td><td><p>Microsoft Visio</p></td><td><p>application/vnd.visio</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.7z</p></td><td><p>7-zip archive</p></td><td><p>application/x-7z-compressed</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.abw</p></td><td><p>AbiWord document</p></td><td><p>application/x-abiword</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.bz</p></td><td><p>BZip archive</p></td><td><p>application/x-bzip</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.bz2</p></td><td><p>BZip2 archive</p></td><td><p>application/x-bzip2</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.csh</p></td><td><p>C-Shell script</p></td><td><p>application/x-csh</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.rar</p></td><td><p>RAR archive</p></td><td><p>application/x-rar-compressed</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.sh</p></td><td><p>Bourne shell script</p></td><td><p>application/x-sh</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.swf</p></td><td><p>Small web format (SWF) or Adobe Flash document</p></td><td><p>application/x-shockwave-flash</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.tar</p></td><td><p>Tape Archive (TAR)</p></td><td><p>application/x-tar</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.xhtml</p></td><td><p>XHTML</p></td><td><p>application/xhtml+xml</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.xml</p></td><td><p>XML</p></td><td><p>application/xml</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.zip</p></td><td><p>ZIP archive</p></td><td><p>application/zip</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.aac</p></td><td><p>AAC audio file</p></td><td><p>audio/aac</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.mid .midi</p></td><td><p>Musical Instrument Digital Interface (MIDI)</p></td><td><p>audio/midi</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.oga</p></td><td><p>OGG audio</p></td><td><p>audio/ogg</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.weba</p></td><td><p>WEBM audio</p></td><td><p>audio/webm</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.wav</p></td><td><p>Waveform Audio Format</p></td><td><p>audio/x-wav</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ttf</p></td><td><p>TrueType Font</p></td><td><p>font/ttf</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.woff</p></td><td><p>Web Open Font Format (WOFF)</p></td><td><p>font/woff</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.woff2</p></td><td><p>Web Open Font Format (WOFF)</p></td><td><p>font/woff2</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.gif</p></td><td><p>Graphics Interchange Format (GIF)</p></td><td><p>image/gif</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.jpeg .jpg</p></td><td><p>JPEG images</p></td><td><p>image/jpeg</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.svg</p></td><td><p>Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG)</p></td><td><p>image/svg+xml</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.tif</p></td><td><p>.tiff Tagged Image File Format (TIFF)</p></td><td><p>image/tiff</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.webp</p></td><td><p>WEBP image</p></td><td><p>image/webp</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ico</p></td><td><p>Icon format</p></td><td><p>image/x-icon</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ics</p></td><td><p>iCalendar format</p></td><td><p>text/calendar</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.css</p></td><td><p>Cascading Style Sheets (CSS)</p></td><td><p>text/css</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.csv</p></td><td><p>Comma-separated values (CSV)</p></td><td><p>text/csv</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.htm</p></td><td><p>.html HyperText Markup Language (HTML)</p></td><td><p>text/html</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.txt</p></td><td><p>Plain Text</p></td><td><p>text/plain</p></td><td><p>TRUE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.3gp</p></td><td><p>3GPP audio/video container</p></td><td><p>video/3gpp audio/3gpp if it doesnât contain video</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.3g2</p></td><td><p>3GPP2 audio/video container</p></td><td><p>video/3gpp2 audio/3gpp2 if it doesnât contain video</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.mpeg</p></td><td><p>MPEG Video</p></td><td><p>video/mpeg</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.ogv</p></td><td><p>OGG video</p></td><td><p>video/ogg</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.webm</p></td><td><p>WEBM video</p></td><td><p>video/webm</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>.avi</p></td><td><p>AVI: Audio Video Interleave</p></td><td><p>video/x-msvideo</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Multipart</p></td><td><p>"multipart/mixed, multipart/alternative, multipart/digest multipart/parallel"</p></td><td><p>FALSE</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Customize Conversion Behavior

You can override the default behavior and configure the agent to forward all payload formats or none
by adding properties to the `conf/mule-agent.yml` file.

For example, you might want to exclude some formats from the external logs if your organization is
concerned about the security of sharing certain types of data.

### Exclude Payloads

To exclude all payloads for a specific application:

```yaml
mule.agent.tracking.service:
  globalTrackingLevel: DEBUG
  enabled: true
  trackedApplications:
    - appName: someApp
      trackingLevel: DEBUG
      payloadExcluded: true
```

To exclude all payloads for all apps:

```yaml
mule.agent.tracking.service:
  globalTrackingLevel: DEBUG
  enabled: true
  globalPayloadExclusion: true
```

### Include Payloads

You might want to convert and forward payload formats that Runtime Manager agent considers not
convertible.

> [!IMPORTANT] If you force conversion for all payload formats, the payload sent might be the
> payload class type, not the payload message content.

To include all payloads for a specific application:

```yaml
mule.agent.tracking.service:
  globalTrackingLevel: DEBUG
  enabled: true
  trackedApplications:
    - appName: someApp
      trackingLevel: DEBUG
      forcePayloadInclusion: true
```

To include all payloads for all apps:

```yaml
mule.agent.tracking.service:
  globalTrackingLevel: DEBUG
  enabled: true
  forceGlobalPayloadInclusion: true
```

## See Also

- [Export Data to External Analytics Tools](sending-data-from-arm-to-external-analytics-software)
- [Runtime Manager API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services)

# Advanced Usage

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

Usually, all you need to do with the Runtime Manager agent is install it and configure it according
to [Install or Update the Runtime Manager Agent](installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent).

If you are curious about the underlying architecture and the advanced configuration settings for the
Runtime Manager agent, explore these topics:

- [Runtime Manager Agent Architecture](runtime-manager-agent-architecture)
- [Agent API](runtime-manager-agent-api)
- [Event Tracking](event-tracking)
- [Internal Handler Buffering](internal-handler-buffering)
- [Extend the Runtime Manager Agent](extending-the-runtime-manager-agent)
- [Application Validator Service](rtm-agent-app-validator)
  - [JAR Signature Validator](rtm-agent-jar-signature-validator)

# Alerts

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo cloudhub 2.0 active](../_/img/icons/clouds.svg "CloudHub 2.0")CloudHub 2.0](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-0)[ ![logo cloudhub active](../_/img/icons/cloudhub.svg "CloudHub")CloudHub ](deployment-strategies#cloudhub)[ ![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid ](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition ](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)[ ![logo rtf active](../_/img/icons/cloud-gear.svg "Runtime Fabric")Runtime Fabric](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

You can set up alerts to send email notifications when application or server events occur.

Runtime Manager includes several standard alert types that trigger notifications for common events
such as:

- An application exceeds a specific number of events processed within a specified period of time.
- A deployment is complete, either successfully or not.
- The worker monitoring system notifies CloudHub about a problem with a worker on an application.
- A server is disconnected or deleted.

> [!NOTE] Runtime Manager retains thirty days of alert history.
>
> Alerts retrigger when the conditions exceed the specified threshold.

You can also create custom alerts for applications deployed to CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0.

Custom alerts in CloudHub are triggered when your application sends notifications to the Runtime
Manager console using Anypoint Connector for CloudHub (CloudHub Connector). See
[Custom Application Alerts](../cloudhub/custom-application-alerts) for information.

For applications deployed to CloudHub 2.0, custom alerts indicate whether an app was successfully
deployed and whether it is behaving as expected. See
[Configuring Application Alerts](../cloudhub-2/ch2-config-app-alerts) for more information.

> [!NOTE] For CloudHub 2.0, Alerts apply to applications only, not servers.

## Rate Limits on Alerts

Anypoint Platform enforces rate limits that determine how often an alert is triggered and prevent
excessive emails from being sent. At present, there is no defined limit to the number of Runtime
Manager alerts.

## Create an Alert

To create an alert:

1.  In Runtime Manager, click **Alerts** in the left navigation pane.
    - If no alerts are set up, you see:

      ![Alerts page with no alerts set up](_images/alerts-no-alerts.png)

      Figure 1. The arrow shows the **Create your first alert** option.

    - If your account already has associated alerts, you see:

      ![Alerts page with blue plus (+) icon](_images/alerts-create-additional-alert.png)

      Figure 2. The arrow shows the blue plus (**+**) icon on the **Alerts** page.

2.  Click the blue plus (**+**) icon to create an alert.

    ![Create an Alert page](_images/alerts-create-alert.png)

3.  Complete the required fields:
    - **Name**

      Unique and meaningful name for the alert

      The **Name** is limited to 40 characters.

    - **Severity level**

      Severity of the alert: **Critical**, **Warning**, or **Info**

    - **Source**

      The source of the alert: applications or servers

      This field appears only if your Anypoint Platform account supports local servers.

    - **Application type**

      When the source is **Applications**, applications deployed to either CloudHub or local
      servers. Runtime Fabric application alerts are supported only using Anypoint Monitoring.

      You canât create an alert that applies to both applications deployed to CloudHub and local
      servers. You must create separate alerts for each deployment type.

    - **Server type**

      When the source is **Servers**, the type of server: single server, server group, or cluster.

      If your servers arenât available on your menu, see
      [Servers, Server Groups, and Clusters](managing-servers).

    - **Applications** or **Servers**

      The application or server to apply the alert to

    - **Condition**

      The condition that triggers the alert

      The available options depend on whether your alert is linked to a server, an application
      running on CloudHub, or an application running locally. Some conditions require additional
      data. See [Alert Conditions](#alert-conditions).

    - **Subject**

      The subject of the alert email to send when the alert is triggered

      The subject can include variables. See [Alert Variables](#alert-variables).

    - **Message**

      The body text for the email to send when the alert is triggered. The message body can include
      variables. See [Alert Variables](#alert-variables).

    - **Recipients**

      The recipients of the alert email

      You can select up to 20 platform user IDs and enter up to 20 email addresses.

4.  Click **Create Alert**.

When an event that meets an alert condition occurs, Runtime Manager sends an email similar to the
following:

![Anypoint Platform Alert Notification email](_images/alerts-alert-email.png)

## Alert Conditions

You can select different conditions depending on the alert source. Some conditions are simple
success or failure notifications, such as `Deployment success`. Some more complex conditions require
that you define parameters that appear as new fields in the form.

### Conditions for Applications Deployed to CloudHub

[![logo cloud active](_images/logo-cloud-active.png "CloudHub")](/runtime-manager/deployment-strategies#cloudhub-deployments)

For applications deployed to CloudHub, the following alert conditions apply:

CPU usage - Cloudhub

The CPU usage of the CloudHub workers that run the application is above or below the percentage for
the specified period of time.

![CPU usage - Cloudhub](_images/alerts-conditions-apps-cpu.png)

To enable this condition, specify the percentage and duration that triggers the alert.

> [!NOTE] For CloudHub multiworker applications, the alert considers the CPU threshold of the entire
> application.

Custom application alert

The custom notification contains the specified text.

![Custom application alerts](_images/alerts-conditions-custom.png)

To enable this condition, specify the notification text from the CloudHub Connector in your Mule
application. When the text in one of the notifications matches the specified string, an alert is
sent. See [Custom Application Alerts](../cloudhub/custom-application-alerts) for more information.

Exceeds event traffic threshold

The number of events of any type exceeds the threshold for the specified period of time.

![Exceeds event traffic threshold](_images/alerts-conditions-exceeds-traffic.png)

To enable this condition, specify the capacity threshold and duration that triggers the alert. This
alert condition depends on the events you are tracking in your application. For information, see
[Business Events](../mule-runtime/latest/business-events).

Memory usage - Cloudhub

Memory usage is a percentage that represents the JVM heap-used/JVM total memory. The alert triggers
if the memory usage is above or below the limit for the specified period of time.

![Memory usage - Cloudhub](_images/alerts-conditions-apps-memory.png)

To enable this condition, specify the percentage and duration that triggers the alert.

The following alerts for applications deployed to CloudHub require no additional parameters:

- Deployment failed
- Deployment success
- Secure data gateway connected
- Secure data gateway disconnected
- Worker not responding

### Conditions for Applications Deployed to Local Servers

[![logo hybrid active](_images/logo-hybrid-active.png "Hybrid")](/runtime-manager/deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)

For applications deployed to local servers, the following alert conditions apply:

Number of errors

The number of messaging errors reported by Mule runtime server in one minute reaches the specified
limit. The number of errors reported depends on error handling configuration in the app. For
information about Mule errors, see [Mule Errors](../mule-runtime/latest/mule-error-concept).

![Number of errors](_images/alerts-conditions-number-errors.png)

To enable this condition, specify the number of errors per minute that triggers the alert.

Number of Mule messages

The number of Mule messages since the application started reaches the specified limit.

![Number of Mule messages](_images/alerts-conditions-number-mule-messages.png)

To enable this condition, specify the number of Mule messages that triggers the alert.

Response time

The response time reaches the specified limit.

![Response time](_images/alerts-conditions-response-time.png)

To enable this condition, specify the maximum response time that triggers the alert.

The following alerts for applications deployed to local servers require no additional parameters:

- Application undeployed
- Deployment failure
- Deployment success

### Conditions for Local Servers

[![logo hybrid active](_images/logo-hybrid-active.png "Hybrid")](/runtime-manager/deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)

The available alert events depend on the server type. The following alerts are available for all
target types (servers and nodes in server groups and clusters):

Server CPU usage

The CPU usage of the servers that run the application is above or below the percentage for the
specified period of time.

![Server CPU usage alert condition](_images/alerts-conditions-servers-cpu.png)

To enable this condition, specify the CPU percentage and duration that triggers the alert.

Server Load average

The average processing load of the server is above or below the amount for the specified period of
time.

![Server load average alert condition](_images/alerts-conditions-servers-load-avg.png)

To enable this condition, specify the average load and duration that triggers the alert.

> [!NOTE] Windows environments donât provide this information, so this metric isnât available in
> Windows environments.

Server Memory usage

The total memory usage (in MB) on the server is above or below the amount for the specified period
of time.

![Server memory usage alert condition](_images/alerts-conditions-servers-memory.png)

To enable this condition, specify the memory usage and duration that triggers the alert.

Server Thread count

The thread count on the JVM is above or below the level for the specified period of time.

![Server thread count alert condition](_images/alerts-conditions-servers-thread-count.png)

To enable this condition, specify the thread count and duration that triggers the alert.

The following alerts vary according to the target type (server, server groups, or clusters) and
require no additional parameters:

Servers

- **Agent version changed**
- **Runtime version changed**
- **Server connected**
- **Server deleted**
- **Server disconnected**

Server Groups

- **Server Group connected**
- **Server Group deleted**
- **Server Group disconnected**
- **Server Groupâs node connected**
- **Server Groupâs node disconnected**
- **Server added to Server Group**
- **Server removed from Server Group**

Clusters

- **Cluster connected**
- **Cluster deleted**
- **Cluster disconnected**
- **Cluster presents visibility issues**
- **Clusterâs node connected**
- **Clusterâs node disconnected**
- **Server added to Cluster**
- **Server removed from Cluster**

## Alert Variables

You can use the following variables in the alert email **Subject** and **Message** fields. For the
description of each variable, see [Variable Definitions](#var-defs).

> [!NOTE] You canât use the combination of characters _$&_.

<table><caption>Table 1. Variables for Alerts</caption> <colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Alert Type</th><th>Alert</th><th>Applicable Variables</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td colspan="2"><div><div><p>All Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${resource}</code> <code>${severity}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="7"><div><div><p>CloudHub App Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>CPU usage - CloudHub</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p><code>${operator}</code> <code>${value}</code> <code>${state}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Memory usage - CloudHub</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Custom application notification</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${user}</code> <code>${priority}</code> <code>${transactionId}</code> <code>${message}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Deployment failed</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${user}</code> <code>${message}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Deployment success</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p><code>${user}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Worker not responding</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Exceeds event traffic threshold</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${period}</code> <code>${periodTimeUnit}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p>Hybrid App Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All Hybrid App Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${name}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Number of Mule messages</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="3"><div><div><p><code>${currentValue}</code> <code>${threshold}</code> <code>${location}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Number of errors</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Response time</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="7"><div><div><p>Server Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All Server Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${name}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Agent version changed</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p><code>${oldVersion}</code> <code>${newVersion}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Runtime version changed</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server CPU usage</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p><code>${currentValue}</code> <code>${comparison}</code> <code>${threshold}</code> <code>${location}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Load average</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Memory average</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Thread count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="9"><div><div><p>Server Group Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All Server Group Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${name}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node CPU usage</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p><code>${memberName}</code> <code>${groupName}</code> <code>${currentValue}</code> <code>${comparison}</code> <code>${threshold}</code> <code>${location}</code> <code>${parentResource}</code> <code>${parentLocation}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node Load average</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node Memory usage</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node Thread count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node connected</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p><code>${memberName}</code> <code>${groupName}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server Groupâs node disconnected</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server added to Server Group</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server removed from Server Group</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="9"><div><div><p>Cluster Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All Cluster Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${name}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node CPU usage</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p><code>${memberName}</code> <code>${clusterName}</code> <code>${currentValue}</code> <code>${comparison}</code> <code>${threshold}</code> <code>${location}</code> <code>${parentResource}</code> <code>${parentLocation}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node Load average</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node Memory usage</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node Thread count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node disconnected</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>${resource}</code> <code>${memberName}</code> <code>${clusterName}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Clusterâs node connected</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="3"><div><div><p><code>${memberName}</code> <code>${clusterName}</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server added to Cluster</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Server removed from Cluster</strong></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table id="var-defs"><caption>Table 2. Variable Definitions</caption> <colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Variable</th><th>Definition</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>${clusterName}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${currentValue}</code></p></td><td><p>Current value of metric that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${comparison}</code></p></td><td><p>Above or Below</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${groupName}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the server group</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${location}</code></p></td><td><p>Where to find more information about the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${memberName}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the node in a server group or cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${message}</code></p></td><td><p>Message from the notification that trigger the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${name}</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the item that triggers the alert:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Hybrid Apps: Name of the app that triggers the alert</p></li><li><p>Servers: Name of the target (server, server group, or cluster) that triggers the alert</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${newVersion}</code></p></td><td><p>New version number for the agent or runtime</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${oldVersion}</code></p></td><td><p>Previous version number for the agent or runtime</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${operator}</code></p></td><td><p>An operator, such as <code>&lt; &gt; =</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${parentLocation}</code></p></td><td><p>Where to find more information about the alert on the nodeâs parent cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${parentResource}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the resource, such as server or app, on the nodeâs parent cluster that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${period}</code></p></td><td><p>Period of time over which to measure</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${periodTimeUnit}</code></p></td><td><p>Period of time unit</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${priority}</code></p></td><td><p>Priority of the notification that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${resource}</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the item that triggers the alert:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CloudHub Apps: Name of the app that triggers the alert</p></li><li><p>Hybrid Apps: Name of the resource in the app that triggers the alert</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${severity}</code></p></td><td><p>Severity of the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${state}</code></p></td><td><p>State of the resource that triggers the alert, such as Running or Disconnected</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${threshold}</code></p></td><td><p>Count over the period of time that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${transactionId}</code></p></td><td><p>Transaction ID defined in the app or assigned by Mule</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${user}</code></p></td><td><p>Anypoint Platform organization name</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${value}</code></p></td><td><p>A value associated with an element</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Access Alerts in Runtime Manager

You can access alerts in Runtime Manager in several ways.

To display all the alerts associated with your account, click **Alerts** in the left navigation
pane:

![Alerts menu](_images/alerts-view-all-alerts-tab.png)

Figure 3. The arrow shows the **Alerts** menu.

From this page, you can:

- Create an alert
- Search for a string in an alert name
- Filter the displayed alerts by source, condition, severity, or status
- Click an alert name to change its settings
- Select an alert to display its status, settings, and history in the details pane:

  ![Alert history in the details pane](_images/alerts-history.png)

  Figure 4. The arrow shows the alert history in the details pane.

To display the alerts associated with an application or server:

1.  In the **Applications** or **Servers** list, click the **Status** column for the application or
    server.
2.  Click the **View Associated Alerts** link in the details pane:

    ![View Associated Alerts link in the details pane](_images/alerts-view-details-pane.png)

    Figure 5. The arrow shows the **View Associated Alerts** link in the details pane.

To display the alert history for a server, server group, or cluster, or an application deployed to a
local server:

1.  In the **Applications** or **Servers** list, click the application or server name.
2.  Click **Settings** in the left navigation pane.
3.  Click the **Alerts History** tab:

    ![Alerts History tab on the Settings page](_images/alerts-settings-alerts-history-tab.png)

    Figure 6. The arrow shows the **Alerts History** tab on the **Settings** page.

## Manage Alerts in Runtime Manager

To manage alerts from the **Alerts** page, select one or more alerts in the list and select an
action in the details pane:

![Select toggle, alert state switch, Duplicate icon, and Delete icon](_images/alerts-multi-select-manage.png)

Figure 7. The screenshot shows (**1**) the select toggle, (**2**) the alert state switch, (**3**)
the **Duplicate** icon, and (**4**) the **Delete** icon.

Switch an alert on or off

All users of Anypoint Platform, even those without permission to create alerts, can control whether
email alerts are sent by switching an alert on or off.

When you change the state of an alert, the change is applied for all users who receive email
notifications for that alert.

- To turn alerts off, click the toggle switch:

  ![Alert switch on](_images/alerts-switch-on.png)

  Figure 8. The arrow shows the alert switch on.

  The value in the **Active** column changes from **Yes** to **No**.

- To turn alerts on, click the toggle switch:

  ![Alert switch off](_images/alerts-switch-off.png)

  Figure 9. The arrow shows the alert switch off.

  The value in the **Active** column changes from **No** to **Yes**.

Create a copy of an alert

Select one or more alerts and click the **Duplicate** icon in the details pane:

![Duplicate alert icon](_images/alerts-duplicate.png)

Figure 10. The arrow shows the **Duplicate** alert icon.

Runtime Manager creates a copy of the alert with `-copy` appended to the name of the original alert.

Delete an alert

Select one or more alerts and click the **Delete** (trash can) icon in the details pane:

![Delete (trash can) icon](_images/alerts-delete.png)

Figure 11. The arrow shows the **Delete** alert (trash can) icon.

When prompted, click **OK** to confirm the deletion.

## See Also

- [Custom Application Alerts](../cloudhub/custom-application-alerts)
- [Monitoring Dashboards](monitoring-dashboards)
- [Monitor Applications](monitoring)
- [Configure Default Events Tracking](../mule-runtime/latest/business-events-in-components)
- [Setting Up Basic Alerts in Anypoint Monitoring (US and EU)](../monitoring/basic-alerts)
---

# Clusters

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

> [!NOTE] For information about how clustering applies to CloudHub 2.0, see
> [Clustering](../cloudhub-2/ch2-clustering).

A cluster is a set of up to eight servers that act as a single deployment target and
high-availability processing unit. Unlike in server groups, application instances in a cluster are
aware of each other, share common information, and synchronize statuses. If one server fails,
another server takes over processing applications. A cluster can run multiple applications.

Before creating a cluster, you must create the Mule runtime engine instances and add the Mule
servers to Anypoint Runtime Manager.

## Multicast vs Unicast Clusters

While configuring a cluster, specify either unicast or multicast options for identifying a node
within the cluster:

- **Unicast**:

  A unicast cluster requires that you configure the IP addresses of the nodes in the cluster. If a
  server has multiple interfaces, use the internal IP address that allows the node to communicate
  directly with other nodes. Clustering across different availability zones or regions can cause a
  cluster performance issue, as it relies on high speed connectivity. Putting all cluster nodes on
  one subnet or in the same availability zone reduces performance risks.

  Because Mule relies on the IP address to identify the server, IP addresses canât be dynamically
  assigned using DHCP for servers on a unicast cluster. If a server is restarted and uses DHCP to
  get a new IP address, you must add the server to the cluster using its new IP address.

  The server status must be Running to be added to a unicast cluster.

- **Multicast**:

  A multicast cluster comprises servers that automatically detect each other. Servers that are part
  of a multicast cluster must be on the same network segment.

  One advantage of multicast clusters is that the server status doesnât need to be Running to
  configure it as a node in a cluster. Another is that you can add nodes to the cluster dynamically
  without restarting the cluster.

  Check with your network administrator to determine if multicast is allowed within your network,
  because many networks block multicast functionalities.

## See Also

- [Add Servers to Runtime Manager](servers-create)
- [Mule High Availability Clusters](../mule-runtime/latest/mule-high-availability-ha-clusters)
---

# Add Servers to a Cluster

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

All servers that you add to a cluster must run on the same versions of:

- Mule runtime
- Runtime Manager agent
- Java

> [!IMPORTANT] When you add a server to a unicast cluster, all existing servers in the cluster are
> restarted.

## Prerequisites

- At least one server is configured.
- The cluster is created.

## Add Servers to a Cluster

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Servers** in the left menu.
3.  Click **Cluster** in the **Type** column to display the details pane:

    ![Add Server button](_images/rtm-cluster-add-server-to-cluster.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the **Add Server** button in the details pane.

4.  Click **Add Server**.

    Servers that are already in a server group or cluster donât appear in the list of available
    servers.

5.  In the list, select the servers to add to the cluster, and click **Add Servers**.

The servers no longer appear in the **Servers** list. To see the list of servers in the cluster,
click the cluster name.

## See Also

- [Multicast vs Unicast Clusters](cluster-about#multicast-vs-unicast-clusters)
- [Add Servers to Runtime Manager](servers-create)
---

# Create Clusters in Runtime Manager

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

To deploy applications to a cluster, add the servers to Runtime Manager first and then create the
cluster. You need to add at least one server to create a cluster.

> [!IMPORTANT] When you add a server to a unicast cluster, all existing servers in the cluster are
> restarted.

For Object Store persistence on all the servers in your cluster, create your cluster manually and
use Quorum Management.

## Restrictions

Before you add servers to a cluster, note the following restrictions:

- Servers cannot contain any previously deployed applications.
- Servers cannot belong to another cluster or group.
- Multicast servers can be in the **Running** or **Disconnected** state.
- Unicast servers must be in the Running state.
- All servers in a cluster must be running the same versions of:
  - Mule runtime engine (including monthly update)
  - Runtime Manager agent
  - Java

    For information about monthly updates, see
    [Monthly Date-Patch Updates](../cloudhub/cloudhub-app-runtime-version-updates#monthly-date-patch-updates).

## Create a Cluster

To create a cluster:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Servers** in the menu.
3.  Click the **Create Cluster** button:

    ![Create Cluster button](_images/rtm-cluster-create.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the **Create Cluster** button on the **Servers** page.

4.  In the **Create Cluster** page, enter the name for the cluster.

    Cluster names can contain between 3-40 alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) and hyphens (-).
    They cannot start or end with a hyphen and cannot contain spaces or other characters.

5.  Select **Unicast** or **Multicast**.
6.  Select the servers to include in your new cluster.
7.  Click the **Create Cluster** button.

The new cluster appears in the **Servers** list. The servers no longer appear in the **Servers**
list. To see the list of servers in the cluster, click the cluster name.

## See Also

- [Clusters](cluster-about)
- [Add Servers to Runtime Manager](servers-create)
- [Object Store Persistence](../mule-runtime/latest/creating-and-managing-a-cluster-manually#object-store-persistence)
- [Quorum Management](../mule-runtime/latest/creating-and-managing-a-cluster-manually#quorum-management)
- [Shut Down, Restart, or Delete Servers](servers-actions)
- [Deployment and Server Scaling](runtime-dep-serv-limits)
---

# Remove Servers from a Cluster

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

You can remove a server from a cluster without changing the server or cluster.

> [!IMPORTANT] When you remove a server from a unicast cluster, all existing servers in the cluster
> are restarted.

To remove a server from a cluster:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Servers** in the left menu.
3.  Click **Cluster** in the **Type** column to display the details pane.
4.  Click the **X** icon to the right of the server to remove the server from the cluster:

    ![X icon](_images/rtm-remove-server-from-cluster.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the **X** icon in the details pane.

5.  In the **Remove** confirmation window, select the checkbox to verify that you want to remove the
    server from the cluster, and then click the **Remove** button.

## See Also

- [Multicast vs Unicast Clusters](cluster-about#multicast-vs-unicast-clusters)
---

# Delete Clusters

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo cloudhub 2.0 active](../_/img/icons/clouds.svg "CloudHub 2.0")CloudHub 2.0](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-0)[ ![logo cloudhub active](../_/img/icons/cloudhub.svg "CloudHub")CloudHub ](deployment-strategies#cloudhub)[ ![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid ](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition ](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)[ ![logo rtf active](../_/img/icons/cloud-gear.svg "Runtime Fabric")Runtime Fabric](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

When deleting a cluster, consider the following:

- Alert definitions and history for the cluster are deleted.
- Servers assigned to the cluster are not deleted.
- Servers assigned to the cluster are restarted.
- Applications deployed to the servers in the cluster are removed.

To delete a cluster:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Select **Servers** in the left menu.
3.  Click **Cluster** in the **Type** column to display the details pane.
4.  Click the cluster status and select **Delete** from the menu:

    ![Delete option](_images/rtm-cluster-delete.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the **Delete** option in the details pane.

5.  In the confirmation window, select the checkbox to verify that you want to delete the cluster,
    and then click the **Delete Cluster** button.

After deleting the cluster, the servers that were in that cluster are restarted.

## See Also

- [Multicast vs Unicast Clusters](cluster-about#multicast-vs-unicast-clusters)
- [Remove Servers from a Cluster](cluster-del-srv)
---

# Continuous Deployment

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo cloudhub 2.0 active](../_/img/icons/clouds.svg "CloudHub 2.0")CloudHub 2.0](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-0)[ ![logo cloudhub active](../_/img/icons/cloudhub.svg "CloudHub")CloudHub ](deployment-strategies#cloudhub)[ ![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid ](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition ](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)[ ![logo rtf active](../_/img/icons/cloud-gear.svg "Runtime Fabric")Runtime Fabric](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

You can use the Runtime Manager REST API for continuous deployment to your own on-premises servers,
or to other cloud servers (outside of CloudHub). The Runtime Manager REST API enables you to
programmatically access much of the functionality provided by Runtime Manager. If you want to
implement continuous deployment to CloudHub, see [CloudHub REST API](../cloudhub/cloudhub-api).

You can implement the process described here as a task in the continuous deployment pipeline in the
following ways:

- As a shell (for example, Microsoft PowerShell, Bash, and so on)
- As a script
- As a Jenkins/Maven Plugin written in Java

For a more complete reference of all the operations, resources, methods, required properties, and
expected responses that are supported through the API, see
[Standalone Runtime Manager API Reference](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services)
or
[Runtime Fabric API Reference](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/runtime-fabric/)

A Mule Maven plugin that provides the same capabilities is also available. Therefore, use the
process described here as an alternative if you cannot, or do not want, to use the
[Mule Maven Plugin](../mule-runtime/latest/mmp-concept).

It is advisable to create a dedicated Anypoint Platform account used exclusively for deployment and
CI related activities.

## Workflow for On-Premises Servers

The workflow explained below can be carried out by various means, depending on your preference, but
the steps remain the same. The high-level steps are as follows:

- The user is signed into Anypoint Platform.
- Information about the servers, server groups, and clusters that are registered on that account is
  obtained.
- An application is deployed to it.
- The application interfaces with the APIs for access management and Runtime Manager.

The workflow must receive the following input parameters:

- **Username** - The Anypoint Platform account username
- **Password** - Password for the Anypoint Platform username
- **Environment** - The deployment environment (for example, Dev, Test, Prod)
- **Target Name** - The name of the target to which to deploy the application
- **Target Type** - The type of target (Server, Cluster, ServerGroup, Runtime Fabric)
- **Artifact Name** - Name of the Mule application to deploy
- **File** - The binary file of the Mule application to deploy

The workflow executes the following API calls in sequence:

![workflow](_images/arm-api-workflow.png)

To use the
[Anypoint Platform Access Management API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/access-management-api/):

1.  Call `/login` on the Access Management API with a valid Anypoint Platform user name and password
    to obtain the `access_token` and `token_type` to use in all the subsequent calls.

    > [!TIP] If using Jenkins, the **Username** and **Password** must be configured in the task. The
    > task has to concatenate the `token_type` and `access_token` and save them for the duration of
    > the entire workflow process. The fields must be concatenated as follows:
    > `token = token_type + " " + access_token`, for example:
    > `"bearer 895d1a05-ba24-44f2-a9df-3675c1bde97b"`.  
    > The token in its concatenated form must be passed as a header in every subsequent API call.

2.  Call `/accounts` on the Access Management API to retrieve the organization `id`.

    > [!TIP] The task must then extract the organization identifier from the location
    > 'user.organisation.id' and save it for later use. This step is not mandatory, as the
    > organization identifier can be passed as a local parameter to the task due to its static
    > nature.

3.  Call `/environments` on the Access Management API, passing the organization `id` retrieved in
    the previous step and choosing the correct environment `id` based on the relevant environment
    name (for example, `Dev`, `Test`, `Production`, and so on).

    > [!TIP] The organization `id` retrieved in Step 2 must be passed as part of the API URI. The
    > environment `id` must be extracted from the path 'data\[i\].id', where 'data\[i\].name ==
    > inputEnvironment' (it can take values like 'Dev', 'Test', 'Production' or any valid
    > environment name that is set up in your Anypoint Platform).

To use the
[Standalone Runtime Manager API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services):

1.  Call either `/servers`, `/clusters`, or `/serverGroups` on the Runtime Manager API, passing the
    server, cluster, or server group name, as well as the retrieved organization `id` and
    environment `id`. This call returns all of the data about this server, cluster, or server group,
    including the `targetId`.

    > [!NOTE] This step retrieves the target `id`, which is then used as a target for the
    > deployment. The task needs to pick up the right server, server group, or cluster identifier
    > based on the target name you set as an input at the beginning of the workflow. The server `id`
    > must be extracted from `data[i].id`, where `data[i].name == inputTargetName`.

2.  Call `/applications` on the Runtime Manager API, passing the organizationâs `id` and the
    environmentâs `id`, to retrieve the applicationâs `id` and the rest of its data.

    > [!NOTE] This step retrieves the application `id` to determine whether the following step is a
    > new deployment (6a) or a re-deployment (6b). The application `id` must be extracted from
    > 'data\[i\].id' where 'data\[i\].artifact.name == inputArtifactName' and 'data\[i\].target.id
    > == serverId' / 'clusterId' / 'serverGroupId'.

3.  Post `/applications` on the Runtime Manager API, passing the organizationâs `id`, the
    environmentâs `id`, 'artifactName', `targetId` (retrieved in Step 1 of this section), the Mule
    application .zip file to deploy and, optionally, the application `id`.

> [!NOTE] This step deploys the actual Mule application artifact for the first time to a target
> environment and server / cluster / server group. Use **POST** requests if no application
> identifier was retrieved in Step 2. Use **PATCH** if an application identifier was retrieved in
> Step 2, to re-deploy the actual Mule application artifact to a target environment and server /
> cluster / server group.

## Workflow for On-Premises Runtime Fabric

The workflow explained below can be carried out by various means depending on your preference, but
the steps remain the same. The high-level steps are as follows:

- It first logs your user into the Anypoint Platform
- Information about the Runtime Fabrics that are registered on that account is obtained
- An application is deployed to it
- The application interfaces with the APIs for the MuleSoft Anypoint Platform Access Management and
  Runtime Manager.

The workflow must receive the following input parameters:

- **Username** - The Anypoint Platform account username
- **Password** - Password for the Anypoint Platform username
- **Environment** - The deployment environment (Dev, Test, Prod, etc.)
- **Target Settings** - The type (ie: MC), name of the target to which to deploy the application,
  and settings
- **Exchange Reference** - Reference of the Mule application to deploy in Exchange
- **Application Configuration** - The application configurations for the deployment

The workflow executes the following API calls in sequence.

**Using the Maven Tool**

1.  Publish the artifact to the Exchange Assets Manager using
    [Maven](https://docs.mulesoft.com/exchange/to-publish-assets-maven).

    > [!NOTE] This step is required to ensure high availability when failure occurs in the target
    > and the Control Plane needs to re-deploy artifacts for recovery.

To use the
[Anypoint Platform Access Management API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/access-management-api/):

1.  Call `/login` on the Access Management API with a valid Anypoint Platform user name and password
    to obtain the `access_token` and `token_type` to use in all the subsequent calls.

    > [!TIP] If using Jenkins, the **Username** and **Password** must be configured in the task. The
    > task has to concatenate the `token_type` and `access_token` and save them for the duration of
    > the entire workflow process. The fields must be concatenated as follows:
    > `token = token_type + " " + access_token` for example:
    > `"bearer 895d1a05-ba24-44f2-a9df-3675c1bde97b"`.

The token, in its concatenated form, must be passed as a header in every subsequent API call.

1.  Call `/accounts` on the Access Management API to retrieve the organization `id`.

    > [!TIP] The task must then extract the organization identifier from the location
    > 'user.organisation.id' and save it for later use. This step is not mandatory, as the
    > organization identifier can be passed as a local parameter to the task due to its static
    > nature.

2.  Call `/environments` on the Access Management API, passing the organization `id` retrieved in
    the previous step and choosing the correct environment `id` based on the relevant environment
    name (for example, `Dev`, `Test`, `Production`).

    > [!TIP] The organization `id` retrieved in Step 2 must be passed as part of the API URI. The
    > environment `id` must be extracted from the path 'data\[i\].id', where 'data\[i\].name ==
    > inputEnvironment' (it can take values like 'Dev', 'Test', 'Production', or any valid
    > environment name that is set up in your Anypoint Platform).
    - Using the
      [Application Manager API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/amc-application-manager/):

3.  Call deployments on the Application Manager API, passing the organizationâs `id` and the
    environmentâs `id`, to retrieve the applicationâs `id` and the rest of its data.

    > [!NOTE] This step retrieves the application `id` to determine whether the following step is a
    > new deployment (6a) or a re-deployment (6b). The application `id` must be extracted from
    > `data[i].id`, where
    > `` data[i].artifact.name == inputArtifactName' and `data[i].target.targetId == `targetId` ``.

4.  Post `/deployments` on the Application Manager API, passing the organizationâs `id`, the
    environmentâs `id`, `applicationName`, `labels`, `target` deployment settings (including the
    target id retrieved in Step 1 of this section), the Exchange reference of the published Mule
    application 'application' to deploy, the application `configuration`, and optionally the
    application `id`.

> [!NOTE] This step deploys the actual Mule application artifact for the first time to a target
> environment and Runtime Fabric. Use `POST` requests if no application identifier was retrieved in
> step 2. Use `PATCH` if an application identifier was retrieved in step 2 to re-deploy.
---

# Troubleshoot the Runtime Manager Agent

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

Connection issues between your on-premises Mule runtime engine and the Runtime Manager agent might
cause discrepancies, such as metrics not appearing in the server monitoring dashboard in Runtime
Manager.

> [!NOTE] `$MULE_HOME` is environment variable that specifies the location where you installed Mule.
> These instructions use paths for Mac or Linux. If you are using Windows, replace the `$MULE_HOME`
> path with `%MULE_HOME%`.

## Troubleshoot Connection Issues Between the Agent and Mule

To troubleshoot connection issues:

1.  Check the JVM you are using.

    If you are using the IBM JVM, you must use a different truststore file than the default
    truststore installed by the Runtime Manager agent.

2.  Ensure you are using the latest agent version.
    1.  Check the agent version.

        In the Runtime Manager navigation menu, select **Servers** and then click the server name.
        The agent version appears on the **Dashboard** page:

        ![Runtime Manager agent version on the server Dashboard page](_images/rtm-agent-version.png)

        Figure 1. The arrow shows the Runtime Manager agent version on the server **Dashboard**
        page.

    2.  Check the
        [Runtime Manager Agent Release Notes](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-release-notes)
        to determine the latest agent version compatible with your Mule version.
    3.  Update the agent, if necessary.

        See [Update the Agent](installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent#update-the-agent).

3.  Verify that the license and certificate are not expired.
    1.  In the Runtime Manager navigation menu, select **Servers** and then click the server name.
        The expiration dates appear on the **Dashboard** page:

        ![Expiration dates on the server Dashboard page](_images/rtm-server-expirations.png)

        Figure 2. The arrow shows the expiration dates on the server **Dashboard** page.

        To view the dashboard for a server in a server group or cluster, click the server name in
        the details pane.
        - If the license has expired, see
          [Install an Enterprise License](../mule-runtime/latest/installing-an-enterprise-license).
        - If the certificate has expired, see [Renew Server Certificates](servers-cert-renewal).

4.  Verify connectivity with all required hosts.
    1.  Ensure that you allow access to required hosts and ports for Anypoint Platform services.

        See [Ports, IP Addresses, and Hostnames to Allow](rtm-agent-allowlists).

    2.  Verify that your firewall or proxy server is not intercepting the Runtime Manager
        certificate.

        See
        [Verify That the Proxy Server Does Not Modify the Runtime Manager Certificate](rtm-agent-proxy-config#verify-that-the-proxy-server-does-not-modify-the-runtime-manager-certificate).

5.  Verify that the `mule-agent.yml` file is not corrupted or empty.

    The `mule-agent.yml` file contains configuration information for the agent. If the
    `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml` file contains non-ASCII characters or is empty:
    - If you have a backup, use it to restore the corrupted `mule-agent.yml` file.
    - If you donât have a backup, follow these steps:
      1.  Remove the Mule server from Runtime Manager.

          See [Shut Down, Restart, or Delete Servers](servers-actions).

      2.  Uninstall the agent.

          See
          [Uninstall the Agent](installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent#uninstall-the-agent).

      3.  Update the agent to the latest supported version.

          See the
          [Runtime Manager Agent Release Notes](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-release-notes)
          and [Update the Agent](installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent#update-the-agent).

      4.  Add the Mule server to Runtime Manager.

          See [Add Servers to Runtime Manager](servers-create).

## Collect Agent Installation Information for Support

You can use the `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_debug` script to collect information used by Support to
troubleshoot the Anypoint Runtime Manager agent installation issues. If following the
troubleshooting steps doesnât solve your problem with the Runtime Manager agent, run the script and
save the output before filing a support ticket.

The `amc_debug` script performs the following actions:

1.  Discovers the Mule agent JVM process
2.  Extracts the thread dump (not forced if possible)
3.  Optionally extracts the heap dump (if you specify the `-heap` option)

    This step can cause delays in running applications.

4.  Extracts the Java version, CPU usage, memory usage, and disk usage
5.  Copies all logs
6.  Copies the configuration files for the Runtime Manager agent
7.  Archives all the information collected in a ZIP file

### Run the amc_debug Script

To run the `amc_debug` script to collect information about the agent installation:

1.  Open a terminal window and change to the `$MULE_HOME/bin` folder.
2.  Run the script:

    `./amc_debug`

    Include the `-heap` option to extract the heap in addition to the other data.

    After the script finishes, the folder `$MULE_HOME/bin` contains a ZIP file with a name similar
    to `snapshot_202103161626.zip`.

3.  Send the ZIP file to Support.

> [!NOTE] The `amc_debug` script is currently available on Linux/Unix-based systems only.

## See Also

- [Install or Update the Runtime Manager Agent](installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent)
- [Event Tracking](event-tracking)
- [Runtime Manager Agent Notifications](runtime-manager-agent-notifications)
- [Extend the Runtime Manager Agent](extending-the-runtime-manager-agent)
- [Internal Handler Buffering](internal-handler-buffering)
---

# Deploy to Your Servers

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

You can deploy a Mule application to a local server using:

- [Anypoint Platform Runtime Manager cloud console](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-deployments)
- [Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition Runtime Manager console](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-pce-deployments)
- [Runtime Manager API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services)
- [Mule Maven plugin](../mule-runtime/latest/deploy-on-premises)

When you use Runtime Manager to manage a server, you cannot use other deployment and management
methods or tools to manage it.

## Prerequisites

Before you can deploy to a local server, you must first:

1.  [Download and install Mule](../mule-runtime/latest/runtime-installation-task).
2.  [Add a server to Runtime Manager](servers-create).

To copy an application from a non-production (Sandbox) environment to a production environment,
ensure that you have permission on both environments.

To avoid compatibility issues, also ensure that:

- The Mule versions on the servers in both environments are the same or compatible.
- The deployment target type (single server, server group, or cluster) in both environments is the
  same.
- All servers in a server cluster are on the same Java version, and the target Java version matches
  that of the application. You can check the Java version associated to your target in **Anypoint
  Platform** > **Runtime Manager** > **Servers**.

## Deploy an Application from Runtime Manager

To deploy an app to your local server from Runtime Manager:

1.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Select **Runtime Manager**.
3.  In the **Applications** page, click **Deploy application**.
4.  Provide a name for your application.

    The application name identifies your application in Runtime Manager and must be unique across
    environments. Runtime Manager alerts you if the name is already in use.

    Application names can contain between 3 and 42 alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) and
    dashes (-). They cannot contain spaces or other characters.

    > [!IMPORTANT] You cannot change the name of an app after you deploy it. To change the name, you
    > must delete and redeploy the app using the new name.

5.  Select the deployment target.

    The target must be a running server, server group, or cluster.

6.  Specify the application file:
    - Import a file from Exchange:
      1.  Click **Choose file** and then select **Import file from Exchange**.
      2.  Select **Application** for the type.
      3.  Enter the name of your application in the search box.
      4.  Select the application to deploy, select a version from the **Version** menu, and then
          click **Select**.

    - Upload an application ZIP file:
      1.  Click **Choose file** and then select **Upload file**.
      2.  Select the file to upload and then click **Open**.

    - Copy an application from a non-production environment:
      1.  Click **Get from sandbox**.

          This button is available only if a non-production environment exists.

      2.  Select the environment, such as Sandbox, from which to get the application.
      3.  If necessary, search for the application to deploy.
      4.  Select the application and then click **Apply**.

          Only applications that are deployed to a local server, server group, or cluster appear in
          the list. Applications deployed to CloudHub can only be promoted to run on other CloudHub
          workers.

7.  Click tabs to configure application options:

    ![Configuration tabs on the Deploy Application page](_images/rtm-deploy-to-own-servers.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the configuration tabs on the **Deploy Application** page.

    **Deployment Target** tab

    The server, server group, or cluster that you selected determines the Mule version.

    Ensure that this Mule version is the same version used to develop your application.

    **Insight** tab

    Specify metadata options for the Insight analytics feature. See [Insight](insight).

    **Properties** tab

    Specify properties (key-value pairs) that the application uses during deployment and while
    running.

    When you deploy an application from a non-production environment (**Get from sandbox**), the new
    application inherits any properties defined in the original application. You can update these
    properties as required on the **Properties** tab before deploying the application.

    See [Manage Properties for Applications On-Premises](hybrid-manage-props) and
    [Safely Hide Application Properties](../cloudhub/secure-application-properties).

    **Logging** tab

    Configure the logging level for the application: `TRACE`, `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARN`, `ERROR`, or
    `FATAL`.

    Alternatively, you can configure logging by modifying the `level` value in the `log4j2.xml`
    file. See
    [Configuring Custom Logging Settings](../mule-runtime/latest/logging-in-mule#configuring-custom-logging-settings)
    and [Apache Logging Services](https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/configuration.html).

    After you deploy the application, two additional tabs are available:

    **Flow Monitoring** tab

    Specify to make flow monitoring metadata available in the applications **Flows** page.

    **Alerts History** tab

    View alerts for the application.

8.  Click **Deploy Application**.

The new application appears in the list of deployed applications in the Applications tab.

## Change a Deployed Application

You can change the application file of a deployed application:

1.  Select your application in the **Applications** page to open its corresponding panel.
2.  Click **Choose File** to upload a new file.

Your application might experience a moment of downtime while the new version is launched in the
server, server group, or cluster.

## Deployment Errors

If an error occurs and the application cannot be deployed, the application status indicator changes
to `Failed` and the status area indicates that an error occurred. Check the log details for any
application deployment errors. Correct the error, upload the application, and redeploy the
application.

## See Also

- [Ports, IP Addresses, and Hostnames to Allow](rtm-agent-allowlists)
- [Manage Applications on Your Servers](managing-applications-on-your-own-servers)
- [Servers, Server Groups, and Clusters](managing-servers)
- [Monitor Applications and Servers](monitoring)
- [Runtime Manager REST APIs](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services)
---

# Deployment Options

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo cloudhub 2.0 active](../_/img/icons/clouds.svg "CloudHub 2.0")CloudHub 2.0](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-0)[ ![logo cloudhub active](../_/img/icons/cloudhub.svg "CloudHub")CloudHub ](deployment-strategies#cloudhub)[ ![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid ](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition ](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)[ ![logo rtf active](../_/img/icons/cloud-gear.svg "Runtime Fabric")Runtime Fabric](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

Mule applications that run in the Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder IDEs deploy to an
embedded test server in within the IDE. Because this server is not meant for production deployment
and uptime restrictions apply, deploy your Mule applications using one of the deployment options
supported by Anypoint Runtime Manager.

## CloudHub 2.0

CloudHub 2.0 is a fully managed, containerized integration platform as a service (iPaaS) where you
can deploy APIs and integrations as lightweight containers in the cloud.

You can deploy your applications from the [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com) Runtime
Manager cloud console and host them in [CloudHub 2.0](../cloudhub-2/). For more information about
how to deploy applications to CloudHub 2.0, see
[Deploying Apps to CloudHub 2.0](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy).

## CloudHub

CloudHub is a complete integration platform as a service (iPaaS) that provides server functionality
for you to deploy your applications without having to configure a hosting environment. Based on your
contract, you control how many resources to assign to your application.

You can deploy your applications from the [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com) Runtime
Manager cloud console and host them in [CloudHub](../cloudhub/). For more information on how to
deploy applications to CloudHub, see [Deploy to CloudHub](../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub).

## Hybrid Deployments

With the hybrid deployment option, you deploy your applications from the Runtime Manager cloud
console to your Mule servers and use Runtime Manager to manage them. This option provides you with
flexibility and control over your on-premises security but requires you to provide the hosting
infrastructure.

To use the hybrid option, you first register your Mule servers with the Runtime Manager agent. Then,
from Runtime Manager, you can optionally add those servers to server groups or clusters to provide
high availability. Finally, you deploy your applications from Runtime Manager to either a server,
server group, or cluster.

For more information on how to deploy applications to your Mule servers, see
[Deploy to Your Servers](deploying-to-your-own-servers).

## Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition

[Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition](../private-cloud/latest/) is a containerized distribution
of the management and engagement capabilities of Anypoint Platform that you host on-premises or in
your organizationâs private cloud environment.

If your organization has strict regulatory or compliance requirements that limit the use of cloud
solutions, you can use Anypoint Platform PCE to deploy and host your applications on-premises.

For more information on how to deploy applications to Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition, see
[Deploy to Your Servers](deploying-to-your-own-servers).

## Anypoint Runtime Fabric Deployments

Anypoint Runtime Fabric is a container service that automates the deployment and orchestration of
Mule applications and API gateways. Runtime Fabric runs within a customer-managed infrastructure on
AWS, Azure, virtual machines (VMs), and bare-metal servers.

Runtime Fabric contains all of the components it requires. These components, including Docker and
Kubernetes, are optimized to work efficiently with Mule runtimes and other MuleSoft services.

To use the Runtime Fabric option, you first create a Runtime Fabric using Runtime Manager. Then, you
install Runtime Fabric on your infrastructure. Finally, you deploy your applications from the
Runtime Manager cloud console to the Runtime Fabric you created.

You can deploy your applications from the [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com) Runtime
Manager cloud console and host them in [Anypoint Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/). For
more information on how to deploy applications to Runtime Fabric, see
[Deploying Mule Apps to Runtime Fabric Using Runtime Manager](../runtime-fabric/latest/deploy-to-runtime-fabric).

## Deployment Option Icons

Icons appear at the begining of each page in the Runtime Manager documentation, indicating the
deployment options to which the content applies.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Deployment Option</th><th>Icon</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CloudHub 2.0</p></td><td><p><span><a href="#cloudhub-2-deployments"><img src="_images/logo-cloud2-active.png" alt="logo cloud2 active" width="24" height="24" title="CloudHub 2.0"></a></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub</p></td><td><p><span><a href="#cloudhub-deployments"><img src="_images/logo-cloud-active.png" alt="logo cloud active" width="24" height="24" title="CloudHub"></a></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hybrid</p></td><td><p><span><a href="#hybrid-deployments"><img src="_images/logo-hybrid-active.png" alt="logo hybrid active" width="24" height="24" title="Hybrid"></a></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition</p></td><td><p><span><a href="#anypoint-platform-pce-deployments"><img src="_images/logo-server-active.png" alt="logo server active" width="24" height="24" title="Anypoint Platform PCE"></a></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Anypoint Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p><span><a href="#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments"><img src="_images/logo-rtf-active.png" alt="logo rtf active" width="24" height="24" title="Runtime Fabric"></a></span></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Runtime Manager Features

For an overview of the Runtime Manager features available with each deployment option, see:

- [High Availability](#high-availability)
- [Java Versions](#java-versions)
- [Load Balancing](#load-balancing)
- [Logging](#logging)
- [Monitoring](#monitoring)
- [Object Store](#object-store)
- [Scheduling](#scheduling)
- [Security Updates](#security-updates)
- [Shared Resource Support](#shared-resource-support)
- [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
- [Horizontal Autoscaling](#horizontal-autoscaling)

### High Availability

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Deployment Option</th><th>Implementation</th><th>More Information</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CloudHub 2.0</p></td><td><p>Via clustering with two or more replicas and Anypoint VPN.</p></td><td><p><a href="../cloudhub-2/ch2-clustering#replica-scale-out">Clustering</a><br><a href="../cloudhub-2/ps-vpn-about#vpn-ha">VPN High Availability</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub</p></td><td><p>Via clustering with two or more workers and Anypoint VPN.</p></td><td><p><a href="../cloudhub/cloudhub-fabric">CloudHub High Availability Features</a><br><a href="../cloudhub/vpn-high-availability">Anypoint VPN High Availability</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hybrid</p></td><td><p>Via server groups or clusters. Clustered Mule instances have distributed shared memory, which provides persistent VM queues, transactions, and cluster-wide data storage.</p></td><td><p><a href="server-group-about">Server Groups</a><br><a href="cluster-about">Clusters</a><br><a href="../mule-runtime/latest/mule-high-availability-ha-clusters">Mule Runtime High Availability (HA) Cluster Overview</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Private Cloud Edition</p></td><td><p>Via server groups or clusters. Clustered Mule instances have distributed shared memory, which provides persistent VM queues, transactions, and cluster-wide data storage.</p></td><td><p><a href="server-group-about">Server Groups</a><br><a href="cluster-about">Clusters</a><br><a href="../mule-runtime/latest/mule-high-availability-ha-clusters">Mule Runtime High Availability (HA) Cluster Overview</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>Via automatic application fail-over. You obtain high availability when you deploy two or more replicas per application.</p></td><td><p><a href="../runtime-fabric/latest/deploy-to-runtime-fabric">Deploying Mule Apps to Runtime Fabric Using Runtime Manager</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Java Versions
