  Track more granular events by enabling default event tracking for your entire flow or for specific
  message processors or endpoints within your flow. When you configure the flows in your
  application, you can explicitly configure the scope for default event tracking. See
  [Configure Default Events Tracking](../mule-runtime/latest/business-events-in-components).

## Customize the Transaction ID

For transaction-level events, Insight might display long numeric values in the ID column. You can
customize the transaction IDs to display more intuitive values that are relevant to your business
requirements.

To do this, use a Mule expression to configure your Mule application to return meaningful
information about a transaction, such as order number, tracking number, or employee identification
number. See
[Customize the Transaction ID](../mule-runtime/latest/business-events-in-components#customize-the-transaction-id).

## Enable Trace Data Collection

Traces enable you to understand the path of a transaction in the MuleSoft ecosystem by providing
full visibility into the execution path of API calls or application transactions.

When you enable trace data collection for an app, Anypoint Platform collects and exposes tracing
information from Mule runtime engine in the OpenTelemetry standard, enabling you to export it using
the Anypoint Monitoring Distributed Tracing Experience.

You can enable trace data collection for your CloudHub applications running on Mule version 4.6 or
later.

To enable trace data collection for an app:

1.  In Runtime Manager, click the **Runtime** tab.
2.  Select the **Enable tracing** checkbox.
3.  Click **Deploy Application** if you are enabling the feature for a new deployment, or **Apply
    Changes** if you are editing an existing application.

After you enable tracing and redeploy your application, the Runtime Manager backend starts
instrumenting traces for your application.

## See Also

- [Monitor Applications](monitoring)
- [Develop Applications for CloudHub](../cloudhub/developing-applications-for-cloudhub)
- [Deploy to CloudHub](../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub)
- [Manage Deployed Applications](managing-deployed-applications)
- [Manage Applications on CloudHub](../cloudhub/managing-applications-on-cloudhub)
- [CloudHub Insight on the MuleSoft Blog](https://blogs.mulesoft.com/dev-guides/how-to-tutorials/announcing-cloudhub-insight-real-time-visibility-message-replay-and-root-cause-analysis/)
- [Business Events](../mule-runtime/latest/business-events)
---

# Install or Update the Runtime Manager Agent

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

The Anypoint Runtime Manager agent registers Mule runtime engine (Mule) with Runtime Manager. After
Mule is registered, you can manage it using Runtime Manager within the specific environment and
Anypoint Platform business group in which Mule was registered. You cannot register Mule with
multiple Runtime Manager business groups or environments.

Use the `amc_setup` command to install and configure the agent, using different parameters depending
on the type of console to which you are connecting the agent:

- [Anypoint Platform Runtime Manager cloud-based console](#register-mule-with-the-cloud-based-runtime-manager)
- [Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition Runtime Manager console](#register-mule-with-anypoint-platform-pce-runtime-manager)

When you run `amc_setup`, the agent creates the `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml` with the
configuration options you specified.

After initial installation, you can:

- Rerun `amc_setup` to change the agent configuration or to update the agent version.
- Modify `mule-agent.yml` to change the agent configuration.

  Some configuration options, such as extending JMX monitoring to other external services, are not
  possible using `amc_setup`. You must manually add them to `mule-agent.yml`. See
  [Update Agent Configuration in mule-agent.yml](rtm-agent-config-yaml).

> [!NOTE] `$MULE_HOME` is environment variable that specifies the location where you installed Mule.
> These instructions use paths for Mac or Linux. If you are using Windows, replace the `$MULE_HOME`
> path with `%MULE_HOME%`.

## Requirements and Restrictions

- Do not register a single Mule runtime engine with more than one Runtime Manager business group or
  environment.
- Do not register Mule with Runtime Manager more than once.
- Do not register a Mule runtime engine with both an older Mule Management Console (MMC) and Runtime
  Manager.

  If Mule is currently managed in MMC, first unregister it with MMC before running `amc_setup`.

  MuleSoft support provides migration scripts to migrate from MMC to Runtime Manager.

> [!NOTE] Runtime Manager agent canât run in a separate JVM.

## Compatibility

The version of Runtime Manager agent must support the version of Mule and Runtime Manager you are
using. To determine compatibility, check the
[Runtime Manager Agent Release Notes](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-release-notes).

### Runtime Manager Agent Compatibility

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Mule version</th><th>Runtime Manager Agent version</th><th>Java version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>4.3.0</p></td><td><p>2.4.3 or later</p></td><td><p>8</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.4.0</p></td><td><p>2.4.27 or later</p></td><td><p>8</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.4.0</p></td><td><p>2.4.28 or later</p></td><td><p>8,11</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.5.0</p></td><td><p>2.5.6 or later</p></td><td><p>8,11</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.6.0</p></td><td><p>2.6.0 or later</p></td><td><p>8,11,17</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] Using a Runtime Manager Agent version earlier than the ones provided in the compatibility
> table can result in incompatibilities or introduce inconsistencies.

For more information on Java compatibility, see [Java Support](../general/java-support).

## Prerequisites

- Your enterprise license is current.
- If you must download the agent, you must have an Enterprise support account.

## Install the Agent from a Mule Installation

The agent is bundled with the Mule runtime engine installation.

To install the agent:

1.  [Download and install Mule](../mule-runtime/latest/runtime-installation-task).
2.  Run `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup` and specify parameters for your installation.

    See [amc_setup Parameters](#amc_setup-parameters).

## Install the Agent From a .zip File

If you need a different version of the agent than the version included in the software distribution,
you can download it from the Support Portal.

### Download the Agent from the Support Portal

To verify compatibility with your Mule runtime engine and Runtime Manager, check the corresponding
[Runtime Manager Agent Release Notes](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-release-notes).

To download the agent:

1.  Open [MuleSoft Help Center](https://help.mulesoft.com/s/).
2.  Click the **Support** tile.
3.  Click **Login** in the upper right and log in using your Anypoint Platform credentials.

    > [!IMPORTANT] You must have an Enterprise support account to download the agent.

4.  In the MuleSoft Support page, click the **Downloads** tab.
5.  Click **Mule Agent**.
6.  Locate the agent to install and click **Download** in the Action column.

### Install the Agent

To install the agent from the downloaded .zip file:

1.  Stop Mule or the API gateway runtime.
2.  Extract the downloaded `agent-setup-_VERSION_.zip` file to `$MULE_HOME/bin`.

    If prompted, overwrite any conflicting files.

3.  Run `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup` and specify parameters for your installation.

    See [amc_setup Parameters](#amc_setup-parameters).

4.  Restart Mule or the API gateway runtime.

## Update the Agent

If you previously installed the agent with an earlier version of `amc_setup`, you can update the
agent and retain your existing agent configuration in `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml`.

If you keep your `mule-agent.yml` file, ensure that these changes are applied:

- When upgrading to versions 1.12.0 and later:

  See
  [Upgrade Requirements](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-1.12.x-release-notes#1-12-0).

- When upgrading to versions 2.2.0 and later:

  See
  [Upgrade Requirements](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-2.2.x-release-notes#2-2-0).

- When upgrading to versions 2.7.0 and later:

  See
  [Upgrade Requirements](../release-notes/runtime-manager-agent/runtime-manager-agent-2.7.x-release-notes#2-7-0).

To update an existing Runtime Manager agent:

1.  Stop Mule or the API gateway runtime.
2.  Extract the downloaded `agent-setup-_VERSION_.zip` file to `$MULE_HOME/bin`.
3.  When prompted, overwrite any conflicting files.
4.  Run `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup -U`.

    When you run `amc_setup` with the `-U` parameter, you canât include other parameters on the
    command line.

5.  Restart Mule or the API gateway runtime.

If you update the Runtime Manager agent, you must also run the Anypoint Monitoring agent uninstall
script and then re-install the Anypoint Monitoring agent. See
[Update the Anypoint Monitoring Agent](../monitoring/am-installing#update-the-anypoint-monitoring-agent).

### What Happens When You Update the Agent

When you update the agent, the `amc_setup` script:

- Backs up the current version of the agent:
  - Archives the `$MULE_HOME/server-plugins/mule-agent-plugin` to
    `$MULE_HOME/tools/mule-agent-backup.zip`
  - Archives any custom modules (usually located in
    `$MULE_HOME/server-plugins/mule-agent-plugin/lib/modules`) to
    `$MULE_HOME/tools/mule-agent-modules-backup.zip`

- Updates agent libraries in `$MULE_HOME/server-plugins/mule-agent-plugin/lib`
- Retains the current `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml` configuration file
- Leaves modules in `$MULE_HOME/server-plugins/mule-agent-plugin/lib/modules` unchanged

  This directory contains all custom modules added to the agent (not included in the agent
  distribution).

- Updates the default agent truststore name from `truststore.jks` to `anypoint-truststore.jks` (for
  updates to versions 1.11.0 or 2.1.4 and later).

## Roll Back the Agent

If you need to roll back the agent, for example, if problems occur when you update the agent,
restore the previous version from the `mule-agent-backup.zip` file. This ZIP file includes
libraries, configuration files, and compiled classes for the agent.

To roll back the agent:

1.  Stop Mule or the API gateway runtime.
2.  Change to the `mule-agent-plugin` directory:

    `cd $MULE_HOME/server-plugins/mule-agent-plugin`

3.  Ensure that you are in the `mule-agent-plugin` directory:

    `pwd`

4.  Remove the existing agent files:

    `rm -r *`

5.  Copy the backup ZIP file to the `mule-agent-plugin` directory:

    `cp $MULE_HOME/tools/mule-agent-backup.zip .`

6.  Restore the previous version of the agent from the backup ZIP file:

    `unzip mule-agent-backup.zip`

7.  Remove the backup ZIP file:

    `rm mule-agent-backup.zip`

8.  Restart Mule or the API gateway runtime.

## Disable the Agent

To disable the agent:

1.  Stop Mule or the API gateway runtime.
2.  Remove these files:

    ```console
    $MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.jks
    $MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml
    ```

3.  Restart Mule or the API gateway runtime.

> [!NOTE] The agent still starts up when disabled, but it does not perform any operation.

## amc_setup Parameters

Parameters to `amc_setup` enable you to:

- Register Mule with a Runtime Manager console:
  - [Register Mule with the Cloud-Based Runtime Manager](#register-mule-with-the-cloud-based-runtime-manager).
  - [Register Mule with Anypoint Platform PCE Runtime Manager](#register-mule-with-anypoint-platform-pce-runtime-manager).

    Registering Mule with Anypoint Platform PCE requires additional `amc_setup` parameters. See
    [PCE Runtime Manager amc_setup Parameters](#pce-runtime-manager-amc_setup-parameters).

- [Configure the agent to connect through a proxy server](rtm-agent-proxy-config#configure-the-agent-to-connect-through-a-proxy-server).
- [Encrypt passwords in the `mule-agent.yml` file](#encrypt_password).
- [Manage Mule using the local Runtime Manager agent REST API interface](rtm-agent-REST-connections).
- [Update the Agent](#update-the-agent).

To see available options, run `./amc_setup --help`.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>--help</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Displays help for the command on the command line.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-U</code></p></div><div><p><code>--update</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Updates the agent, preserving the existing <code>mule-agent.yml</code> configuration file.</p></div><div><div data-type="IMPORTANT">When you update a previous agent installation using <code>-U</code>, you canât include other parameters on the command line.</div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-E</code></p></div><div><p><code>--encrypt</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encrypts the passwords in an existing <code>mule-agent.yml</code> file.</p></div><div><p>Use this option alone to encrypt passwords. For information, see <a href="#encrypt_password">Encrypt Passwords in an Existing mule-agent.yml File</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>--decrypt</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Decrypts the passwords in an existing <code>mule-agent.yml</code> file, for example, before changing the main password.</p></div><div><p>Use this option alone to decrypt passwords. For information, see <a href="#update-the-main-password">Update the Main Password</a> and <a href="#display-passwords-in-mule-agent-yml-in-plain-text">Display Passwords in mule-agent.yml in Plain Text</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>--mule-home</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>mule-home-directory</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the location of the <code>$MULE_HOME</code> directory.</p></div><div><p>Use this option if you are running the installation script from a location other than <code>$MULE_HOME/bin</code>.</p></div><div><p>The <em>amc_setup</em> script reads the <code>mule-agent.yml</code> file from <code>../conf</code>, relative to the directory specified by <code>--mule-home</code>. For example, if the value of <code>--mule-home</code> is <code>/tmp/Mule/bin</code>, <em>amc_setup</em> reads <code>mule-agent.yml</code> from <code>/tmp/Mule/conf</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>--skip-gateway-clientid</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Skips configuration of the API gateway <code>client_id</code> and <code>client_secret</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-I</code></p></div><div><p><code>--insecure</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the agent to use an unencrypted REST connection.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="rtm-agent-REST-connections#insecure-connection-channel">Insecure Connection Channel</a>.</p></div><div><p>Do not use this parameter with the <a href="#pce-runtime-manager-amc_setup-parameters">PCE <code>amc_setup</code> parameters</a>.</p></div><div><p>This option replaces the contents of <code>$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-S</code></p></div><div><p><code>--secure</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the agent to establish a TLS connection with an on-premises administration console.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="rtm-agent-REST-connections#secure-connection-channel">Secure Connection Channel</a></p></div><div><p>Do not use this parameter with the <a href="#pce-runtime-manager-amc_setup-parameters">PCE <code>amc_setup</code> parameters</a>.</p></div><div><p>This option replaces the contents of <code>$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-H</code></p></div><div><p><code>--hybrid</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>token</em></code></p></div><div><p><code><em>server-name</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the agent to connect with either Anypoint Platform PCE or cloud-based Anypoint Platform managed by MuleSoft.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code><em>token</em></code> is a base64 encoded string that specifies the exact business group and environment with which to register Mule with Runtime Manager.</p></li><li><p><code><em>server-name</em></code> is the instance name used to identify Mule in the Runtime Manager console.</p><div><p>This name must be unique within the business groupâs environment.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-P</code></p></div><div><p><code>--proxy</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>proxy-host</em></code></p></div><div><p><code><em>proxy-port</em></code></p></div><div><p><code><em>proxy-user</em></code></p></div><div><p><code><em>proxy-password</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the proxy configuration to use when registering with the connection.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="rtm-agent-proxy-config">Connect the Agent Through a Proxy Server</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-R</code></p></div><div><p><code>--region</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>region</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the region to deploy to. Available regions are <code>prod</code>, <code>eu1</code>, and <code>us_gov</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>--fips</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables FIPS 140-2 compliance on Mule 4 runtime on *nix-based servers.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Register Mule with the Cloud-Based Runtime Manager

[![logo hybrid active](_images/logo-hybrid-active.png "Hybrid")](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)

To register Mule with the Anypoint Platform Runtime Manager cloud-based console, you need:

- A valid registration token, which identifies a specific environment for a specific business group
  in Anypoint Platform
- An instance name, which identifies Mule in the Runtime Manager console

To register Mule:

1.  In Runtime Manager, click **Servers** in the left menu.
2.  Click the **Add Server** button.
3.  Enter a unique name for your server.

    Server names can contain up to 60 alphanumeric characters (a-z, A-Z, 0-9), periods (.), hyphens
    (-), and underscores (\_), but not spaces or other special characters. Runtime Manager supports
    Unicode characters in server names.

    The server name must be unique in the environment, but it can be the same for the same
    organization in different environments.

    Runtime Manager generates the `amc_setup` command. This command includes the server name you
    specified (`_server-name_`) and the registration token (`_token_`) required to register Mule in
    your environment. The registration token includes your organization ID and the current
    environment.

    ![amc_setup command in the Add Server window](_images/rtm-add-server.png)

    Figure 1. The arrow shows the `amc_setup` command in the **Add Server** window.

4.  Click **Copy command** to copy the command.
5.  In a terminal window:
    1.  Change to the `$MULE_HOME/bin` directory for the Mule instance that youâre registering.
    2.  If you want to encrypt passwords in the `mule-agent.yml` file, set the
        `AGENT_VAR_master_password` environment variable to the main password:

        `export AGENT_VAR_master_password=myPassword`

    3.  Paste the command on the command line.
    4.  Include any other parameters on the `amc_setup` command line.

        See [amc_setup Parameters](#amc_setup-parameters).

        Here is an example `amc_setup` command:

        ```console
        ./amc_setup -H myToken myMuleServer --proxy myProxy-host myProxy-port myProxy-user myProxy-password
        ```

The `amc_setup` command generates the `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml` file.

### Example mule-agent.yml File

Here is an example `mule-agent.yml` file generated by the `-H` option, with encrypted passwords:

```yaml
transports:
  rest.agent.transport:
    enabled: false
  websocket.transport:
    consoleUri: wss://runtime-manager.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443/mule
    handshake:
      enabled: true
      body:
globalConfiguration:
  security:
    keyStorePassword: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,WdPmAQMIC8Atr5iK4phjmbZbs9XFLLiH1eDhA7AmnpKUaPDhp40OB4uKZ6JUMW44]"
    keyStoreAlias: agent
    keyStoreAliasPassword: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,WdPmAQMIC8Atr5iK4phjmbZbs9XFLLiH1eDhA7AmnpKUaPDhp40OB4uKZ6JUMW44]"
  authenticationProxy:
    endpoint: https://data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
  proxyConfiguration:
    password: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,VIs82yFDp66lnsL/Rss8q8js/zPDQ8+HJpdNepdZtA9obeDj5u+z4HIocsbLvXow]"
```

## Register Mule with Anypoint Platform PCE Runtime Manager

[![logo server active](_images/logo-server-active.png "Anypoint Platform PCE")](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-pce-deployments)

With Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition (Anypoint Platform PCE), all Runtime Manager services
run on-premises rather than in a cloud environment managed by MuleSoft.

To register Mule with PCE Runtime Manager:

1.  Log in to an Anypoint Platform PCE account.
2.  Ensure that you have correctly set up the DNS entry for your platform.

    See [DNS or IP](../access-management/private-cloud-edition-features#dns-or-ip).

3.  Select a business group and environment with which to register Mule.
4.  Within the selected environment, click **Servers** in the left menu.
5.  Click the **Add Server** button.

    Runtime Manager generates the `amc_setup` command, including `_token_` and `_server-name_`, to
    use to register Mule in your environment.

    The registration token includes your organizationâs ID and the current environment.

6.  Click **Copy** to copy the command.
7.  In a terminal window, change to the `$MULE_HOME/bin` directory for the Mule instance that youâre
    registering.
8.  Paste the command on the command line.
9.  Change the instance name `server-name` to the unique name to use to label Mule in the Runtime
    Manager console.
10. Include any other parameters on the `amc_setup` command line.

    See [amc_setup Parameters](#amc_setup-parameters).

11. Append parameters to the command line to specify the URLs of services used by Runtime Manager to
    manage your Mule instance.

    See [PCE Runtime Manager amc_setup Parameters](#pce-runtime-manager-amc_setup-parameters).

    Here is an example `amc_setup` command:

    ```console
    ./amc_setup -H myToken myMuleServer -A +http://$DOCKER_IP_ADDRESS:443/hybrid/api/v1+ -W "wss://AnypointPlatformHost:8889/mule" -C https://AnypointPlatformHost/accounts -F https://AnypointPlatformHost/apiplatform
    ```

The `amc_setup` command generates the `$MULE_HOME/conf/mule-agent.yml` file.

### PCE Runtime Manager amc_setup Parameters

This table lists additional parameters required to register Mule with Anypoint Platform PCE Runtime
Manager. Append these parameters to the `./amc_setup -H _token_ _server-name_` command.

Do not use these parameters with the `-I` or `S` parameters to configure REST API connections.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Value</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-A</code></p></div><div><p><code>--amc-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>amc-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location URL of your local instance of Runtime Manager, for example, <code>https://10.0.0.1:443/hybrid/v1</code>.</p></div><div><p>You can test whether the service is available at <code><em>AMC_HOST</em>/hybrid/v1</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-W</code></p></div><div><p><code>--mcm-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>mcm-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location URL of your local instance of MCM, for example, <code>wss://10.0.0.2:8889/mule</code>.</p></div><div><p>You can test whether the service is available at <code><em>MCM_HOST</em>/mule</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-C</code></p></div><div><p><code>--cs-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>core-services-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location URL of your local instance of Access Management, for example, <code>https://10.0.0.3:443/accounts</code>.</p></div><div><p>You can test whether the service is available at <code><em>core-services-host</em>/accounts</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-D</code></p></div><div><p><code>--contract-caching-service-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>contract-caching-service-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location (URL) of your local instance of Contract Caching Service, for example, <code>https://10.0.0.4:8080</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-F</code></p></div><div><p><code>--api-platform-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>api-platform-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location URL of your local instance of API Manager, for example, <code>https://10.0.0.5:443/apiplatform</code>.</p></div><div><p>You can test whether the service is available at <code><em>API_PLATFORM_HOST</em>/apiplatform</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-Z</code></p></div><div><p><code>--auth-proxy-host</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code><em>auth-proxy-host</em></code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service location URL of your Auth Proxy, for example, <code>https://10.0.0.3:8080</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Encrypt Passwords in an Existing mule-agent.yml File

If you didnât set the `AGENT_VAR_master_password` environment variable when registering the agent
with Runtime Manager, you can encrypt the following passwords later in the `mule-agent.yml` file:

- `keystorePassword`: Keystore password used to access the `mule-agent.jks` file
- `keystoreAliasPassword`: Keystore alias password used to access the `mule-agent.jks` file
- `password`: Password used for authenticating with a proxy server

### Prerequisites

- Runtime Manager agent version 2.4.17

  To update previous versions of the agent, see [Update the Agent](#update-the-agent).

### Encrypt Passwords

To encrypt passwords in your `mule-agent.yml` file:

1.  Set the `AGENT_VAR_master_password` environment variable to the main password:

    `export AGENT_VAR_master_password=myPassword`

2.  Run the encryption utility:

    `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup --encrypt`

    The encryption utility replaces the passwords in the `keystorePassword`,
    `keystoreAliasPassword`, and `password` fields in the `mule-agent.yml` file with the encrypted
    passwords, for example:

    ```yaml
    globalConfiguration:
      security:
        keyStorePassword: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,Fe4kl2UBnGRoyh7H2v9lq+QSG7Pe6ZP8k34xmZEHmcAb1jABAwPLk2xWrGtSt1iE]"
        keyStoreAlias: agent
        keyStoreAliasPassword: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,EtqRDPq7UTxDHzZGpOGazfcveEi832Pqm1mqhlwYRGLZGh9NSNXie6nAZr09b3iT]"
      authenticationProxy:
        endpoint: https://data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
      proxyConfiguration:
        password: "![PBEWITHSHA1ANDDESEDE,VIs82yFDp66lnsL/Rss8q8js/zPDQ8+HJpdNepdZtA9obeDj5u+z4HIocsbLvXow]"
    ```

The encrypted passwords start with `!` and are enclosed in square brackets `[ ]`.

### Update and Reencrypt Passwords

To update an encrypted password in the `mule-agent.yml` file:

1.  Edit `mule-agent.yml`.
2.  Replace the encrypted password with the new plain-text password.
3.  Set the `AGENT_VAR_master_password` environment variable:

    `export AGENT_VAR_master_password=myPassword`

4.  Run the encryption utility:

    `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup --encrypt`

### Display Passwords in mule-agent.yml in Plain Text

To display the passwords in `mule-agent.yml` in plain text, run the decryption utility:

`$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup --decrypt`

This command outputs the contents of the `mule-agent.yml` file with plain-text passwords.

### Update the Main Password

To update the main password:

1.  Run the decryption utility:

    `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup --decrypt`

2.  Set the `AGENT_VAR_master_password` environment variable to the new main password:

    `export AGENT_VAR_master_password=myNewPassword`

3.  Run the encryption utility:

    `$MULE_HOME/bin/amc_setup --encrypt`

## See Also

- [Installing an Enterprise License](../mule-runtime/latest/installing-an-enterprise-license)
- [Starting and Stopping Mule](../mule-runtime/latest/starting-and-stopping-mule-esb)
- [Runtime - API Gateway](../mule-gateway/mule-gateway-overview)
- [Runtime Manager Agent Architecture](runtime-manager-agent-architecture)
- [Create a Server in Runtime Manager (Hybrid)](servers-create)
- [Anypoint Private Cloud Installers](../private-cloud/latest/install-workflow)
---

# Internal Handler Buffering

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

The Anypoint Runtime Manager agent provides a simple way of implementing internal handlers with
buffering support. This document explains how to implement and configure buffers for the Runtime
Manager agent internal handlers.

## Implement a Buffered Internal Handler

### BufferedHandler Abstract Class

To implement a buffered internal handler, you need to extend the `BufferedHandler<T>` abstract
class. The methods to overwrite are:

- `boolean canHandle(T message)`

  Returns true if the message can be handled by the internal handler.

- `boolean flush(Collection<T> collectionOfMessages)`

  Provides the implementation to send the messages stored in the buffer to the external service.

### Example

```java
@Named("com.mulesoft.agent.mypublisher")
@Singleton
public class MyPublisher extends BufferedHandler<List<Metric>>
{
    @Configurable(value = "mule")
    String metricPrefix;

    @Inject
    public MyPublisher()
    {
        super();
    }

    public MyPublisher(OnOffSwitch enabledSwitch)
    {
        super();
        this.enabledSwitch = enabledSwitch;
    }

    @Override
    public boolean canHandle(@NotNull List<Metric> metrics)
    {
        // Evaluates if the message could be handled
    }

    @Override
    public boolean flush(@NotNull Collection<List<Metric>> listOfMetrics)
    {
        // Send messages to external service
    }
}
```

## Configure a Buffering Internal Handler

### Buffering Configuration Object

If the internal handler includes buffering support, you can configure the buffer in the
`mule-agent.yml` file. The following table lists the available configuration parameters.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th><th>Default value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>type</code></p></td><td><p>Buffer type. Valid values are <code>MEMORY</code> or <code>DISK</code>.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>retryCount</code></p></td><td><p>Number of retries after an exception was thrown in the flush method.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>maximumCapacity</code></p></td><td><p>Maximum number of events to keep in buffer.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>flushFrequency</code></p></td><td><p>Frequency at which the flush method will run, in milliseconds.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>filePath</code></p></td><td><p>Path to the buffer file. Only valid for buffers of type <code>DISK</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>PATH/bufferFile</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>whenExhausted</code></p></td><td><p>Strategy to follow when the buffer is exhausted. If not specified, the buffer will grow automatically. Valid values are <code>FAIL</code> and <code>FLUSH.</code></p></td><td><p>N/A</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>flushThreadsNumber</code></p></td><td><p>Number of threads used by the FlushThreadExecutor when the buffer is exhausted and the strategy defined by <code>whenExhausted</code> is <code>FLUSH</code> (see row above).</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>discardMessagesOnFlushFailure</code></p></td><td><p>If true, messages will not be returned to the buffer if the flush is not successful.</p></td><td><p><code>false</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!IMPORTANT] The maximum size capacity for the buffer is configured based on a number of events
> and not in bytes. Keep in mind that if the events are of a considerable size and you donât set
> `maximumCapacity` to a low value, the heap might suffer stress and this might impact the
> applicationâs performance.

If you configure a `DISK` buffer, the agent will also instantiate an in-memory buffer in order to
improve the performance of the I/O operations. This memory buffer has a default value of 10000
events and is flushed to disk every 500ms, and in batches of 10000 events each. These values are
configurable through the system properties `agent.disk.buffering.cache.size`,
`agent.disk.buffering.cache.flush.frequency` and `agent.disk.buffering.max.flushing.size`,
respectively.

> [!NOTE] Flushing from disk to the external system in batches of 10000 events each and the
> `agent.disk.buffering.max.flushing.size` property are supported for Mule agent plugin versions
> 2.1.9 and 1.11.3 and later.

### Sample `mule-agent.yml` Files

#### Configure Buffering in a Publisher

```yaml
---
muleInstanceUniqueId: validId
organizationId: organizationId

transports:
  websocket.transport:
    security:
      keyStorePassword: mykeystorePassword
      keyStoreAlias: agent
      keyStoreAliasPassword: agentpassword

  rest.agent.transport:
    security:
      keyStorePassword: mykeystorePassword
      keyStoreAlias: agent
      keyStoreAliasPassword: agentpassword
    port: 9997

services:
  mule.agent.application.service:
    enabled: true

  mule.agent.domain.service:
    enabled: true

  mule.agent.jmx.publisher.service:
    enabled: true
    frequency: 15
    frequencyTimeUnit: MINUTES
    beans:
      - beanQueryPattern: java.lang:type=Runtime
        attribute: Uptime
        monitorMessage: Monitoring memory up-time
      - beanQueryPattern: java.lang:type=MemoryPool,*
        attribute: Usage.used
        monitorMessage": Used Memory

internalHandlers:
  domaindeploymentnotification.internal.message.handler:
    enabled: true

  applicationdeploymentnotification.internal.message.handler:
    enabled: false

  com.mulesoft.agent.test.buffering.jmx.internal.handler:
    enabled: true
    buffer:
      type: DISK
      retryCount: 1
      flushFrequency: 10000
      maximumCapacity: 30
      filePath: publisher-buffer.log

externalHandlers:
  applications.request.handler:
    enabled: true

  domains.request.handler:
    enabled: true
```

#### Configure Buffering in Events Tracking

```yaml
---
muleInstanceUniqueId: validId
organizationId: organizationId

transports:
  websocket.transport:
    security:
      keyStorePassword: mykeystorePassword
      keyStoreAlias: agent
      keyStoreAliasPassword: agentpassword

  rest.agent.transport:
    security:
      keyStorePassword: mykeystorePassword
      keyStoreAlias: agent
      keyStoreAliasPassword: agentpassword
    port: 9997

services:
  mule.agent.application.service:
    enabled: true

  mule.agent.domain.service:
    enabled: true

  mule.agent.jmx.publisher.service:
    enabled: true
    frequency: 15
    frequencyTimeUnit: MINUTES
    beans:
      - beanQueryPattern: java.lang:type=Runtime
        attribute: Uptime
        monitorMessage: Monitoring memory up-time
      - beanQueryPattern: java.lang:type=MemoryPool,*
        attribute: Usage.used
        monitorMessage": Used Memory

internalHandlers:
  domaindeploymentnotification.internal.message.handler:
    enabled: true

  applicationdeploymentnotification.internal.message.handler:
    enabled: false

  tracking.notification.internal.message.handler:
    enabled: true
    buffer:
      type: MEMORY
      retryCount: 1
      flushFrequency: 10000
      maximumCapacity: 30

externalHandlers:
  applications.request.handler:
    enabled: true

  domains.request.handler:
    enabled: true
```
---

# JMX Service

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

The Runtime Manager agent JMX service allows you to track specific JMX metrics and publish them to
external services. The service can be configured to track specific beans and to change the frequency
with which it publishes their value.

> [!NOTE] Mule 4.x doesnât expose beans to JMX.

## JMX Publishers

The JMX service sends all the gathered metrics to publishers, which in turn publish the metrics to
external monitoring tools. The service itself provides some example publishers for commonly-used
tools; furthermore, you can create your own publisher to suit your specific needs.

## Configure the JMX Service

### Operation: Retrieve Configuration

Retrieves the JMX service configuration.

**Request:**

GET <Runtime Manager Agent URL>/mule/agent/mule.agent.jmx.publisher.service HTTP/1.1

**Example:**

GET http://localhost:9999/mule/agent/mule.agent.jmx.publisher.service HTTP/1.1

**Response:**

```json
{
  "serviceHandlerTypes": ["java.util.List<com.mulesoft.agent.domain.monitoring.Metric>"],
  "injectedHandlers": [
    {
      "name": "com.mulesoft.agent.monitoring.publisher.CloudwatchMonitorPublisher",
      "path": "/mule/agent/cloudwatch.agent.monitor.publisher/configuration",
      "type": "java.util.List<com.mulesoft.agent.domain.monitoring.Metric>"
    },
    {
      "name": "com.mulesoft.agent.monitoring.publisher.GraphiteMonitorPublisher",
      "path": "/mule/agent/mule.agent.graphite.jmx.internal.handler/configuration",
      "type": "java.util.List<com.mulesoft.agent.domain.monitoring.Metric>"
    },
    {
      "name": "com.mulesoft.agent.monitoring.publisher.NagiosMonitorPublisher",
      "path": "/mule/agent/mule.agent.nagios.jmx.internal.handler/configuration",
      "type": "java.util.List<com.mulesoft.agent.domain.monitoring.Metric>"
    },
    {
      "name": "com.mulesoft.agent.monitoring.publisher.ZabbixMonitorPublisher",
      "path": "/mule/agent/mule.agent.zabbix.jmx.internal.handler/configuration",
      "type": "java.util.List<com.mulesoft.agent.domain.monitoring.Metric>"
    }
  ],
  "configurableFields": [
    {
      "name": "frequency",
      "valueType": "java.lang.Integer",
      "value": 15,
      "configurableType": "DYNAMIC"
    },
    {
      "name": "frequencyTimeUnit",
      "valueType": "java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit",
      "value": "MINUTES",
      "configurableType": "DYNAMIC"
    },
    {
      "name": "beans",
      "valueType": "[Lcom.mulesoft.agent.services.monitoring.JMXBean;",
      "value": [],
      "configurableType": "DYNAMIC"
    }
  ]
}
```

The response contains a list of the publishers currently loaded, as well as the configurable fields
of the service and their values (for more information on service configuration, see Administration
Service).

### Operation: Modify Configuration

This operation modifies the current configuration of the service.

**Request:**

```json
PATCH <Runtime Manager Agent URL>/mule/agent/mule.agent.jmx.publisher.service HTTP/1.1

{
  "<parameter>": "<value>",
}
```

**Example:**

```json
PATCH http://localhost:9999/mule/agent/myservice HTTP/1.1

{
  "frequencyTimeUnit": "SECONDS",
  "frequency": "150"
}
```

**Response (if successful):**

HTTP 200

### Add a New JMX Publisher

To add a new JMX publisher, implement the Runtime Manager agent `InternalMessageHandler` interface.

Your custom JMX publisher should meet the following requirements:

- It must be thread-safe.
- It must implement all publishing logic in the handle method defined in `InternalMessageHandler`.

#### Write Your JMX Publisher

```java
@Named("my.company.jmx.publisher")
@Singleton
public class MyJMXPublisher<T> extends InternalMessageHandler<List<Metric>>{

    boolean handle(List<Metric> metrics){
          // TODO handle message
    }

    @Override
    public void enable(boolean state) throws AgentEnableOperationException {
        // TODO: enable the Handler
    }

    @Override
    public boolean isEnabled() {
        // TODO: return Handler status
    }
}
```

The Metric class is a POJO that contains the following fields:

```java
/**
 * Time stamp when the metric was taken
 */
long timestamp;

/**
 * Name of the metric. In the case of a JXM metric it is the bean that was tracked + message
 */
String name;

/**
 * The numeric value of the metric
 */
Number value;
```

To add your new JMX publisher, just drop the JAR containing your classes under the `lib` folder
within the Runtime Manager agent plugin.

## See Also

- [Runtime Manager Agent API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/mule-agent-plugin-api/minor/1.1/pages/home/)
---

# Manage Applications on Your Servers

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)

After you [add a server](servers-create) and [deploy an application](deploying-to-your-own-servers)
to it, you can use the Runtime Manager to monitor and manage your application.

Depending on if your application is deployed to [CloudHub](../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub) or to
[your own servers](deploying-to-your-own-servers), there is a different set of possible actions you
can perform on it, once deployed. This page focuses on the actions that are specific to applications
deployed to your own servers. To view the basic management tools that are common to both types of
deployment targets, see [Manage Deployed Applications](managing-deployed-applications).

With applications deployed to your own servers, you can:

- [Monitor applications](monitoring) and the servers they run on
- [Manage servers](managing-servers)

> [!NOTE] This doc covers deployments on-premises that are deployed both through the
> [cloud based](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com) or the Private Cloud Edition of the Anypoint
> Platform. Some of the features described in this page are not yet available on the Anypoint
> Platform Private Cloud Edition. See [Deployment Options](deployment-strategies) for a clear
> distinction. If youâre interested in requesting the Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition,
> please contact your sales representative.

> [!NOTE] MuleSoft does not support manually deploying apps if you are managing your on-premises
> servers with Runtime Manager. If you manually deploy applications on your on-premises servers, you
> canât see or manage them in Runtime Manager because the applications are not auto-discovered.

## Applications Dashboard

If you select any of the applications on the Applications tab, a panel opens. Here you can read
additional information about the application and access its settings.

![managing applications on your own servers 26ef4](_images/managing-applications-on-your-own-servers-26ef4.png)

What this dashboard displays depends on if your application is deployed on
[CloudHub](../cloudhub/managing-applications-on-cloudhub) or on Mule.

Applications deployed to Mule show you the status, the time of the last modification and the
deployed application ZIP file â which you can change from this menu through the **Choose File**
button. Applications deployed on clusters and server groups additionally display the list of servers
and the status of each.

> [!TIP] You can verify info about the servers the application runs on by clicking on the server
> name in the panel, or by searching for it in the **Servers** tab. See more on
> [Servers Dashboard](managing-servers#servers-dashboard).

On all panels, two buttons are displayed:

- The **Manage Application** button, which takes you to the application settings page. This page
  displays more detailed information about your app and the server it runs on. From here you can
  also access the apps dashboard, see
  [Monitoring Dashboards](monitoring-dashboards#the-dashboard-for-apps-on-servers) for more on this.

  ![managing applications on your own servers b0e6f](_images/managing-applications-on-your-own-servers-b0e6f.png)

- The **Insight** button, to see data at a transaction level. See [Insight](insight) for more info
  about what you can see here.

  ![managing applications on your own servers ba927](_images/managing-applications-on-your-own-servers-ba927.png)

  > [!NOTE] The **Insight** feature isnât currently available on the Anypoint Platform Private Cloud
  > Edition.

## Access App Settings

Sign in to the [Anypoint Platform](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com) and view the **Applications** tab
of the Runtime Manager. Select one of your CloudHub running applications to open a panel. On that
panel, click the **Manage Application** button to open up the applicationâs settings.

![managing applications on your own servers 1fd39](_images/managing-applications-on-your-own-servers-1fd39.png)

In this menu you can view all of the same data thatâs available on the panel, with the addition of
an **Alerts History** tab, that displays a record of the triggered alerts on this app. See
[Alerts on Runtime Manager](alerts-on-runtime-manager) for more on this.

## Flow Management

You can individually start and stop the specific flows within your deployed applications via this
tab in the UI. See [Flow Management](flow-management).

## Monitor Applications

If your application is deployed to one of your own servers a set of tools exists for monitoring
events that may occur on your application and the performance of the servers, server groups or
clusters itâs running on. See [Monitoring Applications](monitoring) for more details.

## Delete Applications

To delete a deployed application from a server, simply click on the arrow next to its status, and
select **Delete**.

![managing applications on your own servers 96463](_images/managing-applications-on-your-own-servers-96463.png)

> [!NOTE] The server must be running for this option to be available. In the case of a Cluster or
> Server Group, at least one of its nodes must be running.

### Redeploying Deleted Hybrid Applications

For applications deployed on-premises, a couple of scenarios may arise which trigger specific
behaviors by Runtime Manager:

- If you manually delete an application folder or anchor file while the Mule runtime is running, the
  affected applications are undeployed from that local server. Applications on other nodes in a
  server group or cluster arenât affected.

  > [!NOTE] Only deleting from the Anypoint Runtime Manager UI or via the Runtime Manager REST API
  > permanently removes the application.

- When Mule runtime restarts, it triggers a resync process. During the resync, Runtime Manager
  detects the missing applications and automatically redeploys them. This applies to single servers,
  server groups, and clusters.

  > [!NOTE] The resync only occurs when the local Mule runtime restarts.

- When a new server is added into a Cluster or Server Group, even when no other server in that Group
  or Cluster is running, Runtime Manager automatically deploys any applications into that Server
  Group or Cluster.

## Download an Application Archive

Runtime Manager doesnât support downloading the application archive (ZIP or JAR file) from the
application **Settings** page for an application deployed to an on-premises server. However, you can
download the archive using an Runtime Manager REST Services API call or the Anypoint
[command-line interface](../anypoint-cli/latest/standalone-apps#runtime-mgr-standalone-application-artifact).

## Download CloudHub 2.0, CloudHub, and Runtime Fabric Application Archives

[![logo cloud2 active](_images/logo-cloud2-active.png "CloudHub 2.0")](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-deployments)
[![logo cloud active](_images/logo-cloud-active.png "CloudHub")](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-deployments)
[![logo rtf active](_images/logo-rtf-active.png "Runtime Fabric")](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

You can download the archive file for applications deployed to CloudHub 2.0, CloudHub, and Runtime
Fabric.

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Click the **Applications** tab.
3.  Click the name of the application whose archive you want to download.
4.  Click the name of the archive file in the **Application File** field.

    ![download archive file](_images/download-archive-file.png)

## See Also

- [Servers, Server Groups, and Clusters](managing-servers)
- [Deploy Applications to Your Servers](deploying-to-your-own-servers)
- [Manage Deployed Applications](managing-deployed-applications)
- [Monitor Applications](monitoring)
- [Runtime Manager REST API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/arm-rest-services)
---

# Manage Deployed Applications

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![logo cloudhub 2.0 active](../_/img/icons/clouds.svg "CloudHub 2.0")CloudHub 2.0](deployment-strategies#cloudhub-2-0)[ ![logo cloudhub active](../_/img/icons/cloudhub.svg "CloudHub")CloudHub ](deployment-strategies#cloudhub)[ ![logo hybrid active](../_/img/icons/hybrid-cloud.svg "Hybrid")Hybrid ](deployment-strategies#hybrid-deployments)[ ![logo server active](../_/img/icons/server-cloud.svg "Anypoint Platform PCE")Private Cloud Edition ](deployment-strategies#anypoint-platform-private-cloud-edition)[ ![logo rtf active](../_/img/icons/cloud-gear.svg "Runtime Fabric")Runtime Fabric](deployment-strategies#anypoint-runtime-fabric-deployments)

After you deploy apps, use the Runtime Manager console to monitor and manage running applications.
The different management functionalities available depend on the
[deployment option](deployment-strategies) you use.

The basic management tools in the Runtime Manager console enable you to perform the following tasks:

- [Start, stop, and delete apps](#start_stop_delete_app)
- [View status states for apps](#status_states)
- [Update apps](#update_application)
- [Monitor and troubleshoot apps](monitoring)

For applications deployed to [your own servers](managing-applications-on-your-own-servers), you can
also:

- [Manage servers](managing-servers)
- [Create clusters and server groups](server-group-create)
- [Send data from Runtime Manager to external analytics software](sending-data-from-arm-to-external-analytics-software)
- [Manage individual flows](flow-management)

On applications deployed to [CloudHub 2.0](../cloudhub-2/ch2-manage-apps), you can also:

- [Configure Application Alerts](../cloudhub-2/ch2-config-app-alerts)
- [Manage Application Schedules](../cloudhub-2/ch2-manage-schedules)
- [Add Application Properties](../cloudhub-2/ch2-manage-props)
- [View Log Data for Deployed Applications](../cloudhub-2/ch2-view-logs)
- [View Diagnostic Data for Deployed Applications](../cloudhub-2/ch2-view-diag)
- [Enable Distributed Tracing](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-private-space#enable-distributed-tracing)
- [Use Object Store v2](../cloudhub-2/ch2-clustering#cloudhub-2-0-clustering-and-object-store-v2)

On applications deployed to [CloudHub](../cloudhub/managing-applications-on-cloudhub), you can also:

- [Manage notifications](../cloudhub/notifications-on-runtime-manager)
- [Use Insight to troubleshoot business transactions and events on your Mule apps](insight)
- [Manage queues](../cloudhub/managing-queues)
- [Manage schedules](../cloudhub/managing-schedules)
- [Safely hiding application properties](../cloudhub/secure-application-properties)
- [View application log data](../cloudhub/viewing-log-data)
- [Monitor CloudHub workers](../cloudhub/worker-monitoring)
- [Manage app data with object stores](../cloudhub/managing-application-data-with-object-stores)

On applications deployed to [Anypoint Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/), you can also:

- [Manage App Schedules](../runtime-fabric/latest/manage-schedules)
- [Protect Application Property Values](../runtime-fabric/latest/protect-app-properties)
- [Review Application Versioning](../runtime-fabric/latest/app-versioning)

## Applications Tab

In Runtime Manager, an "application" is a Mule application that is running on a Mule runtime engine
(Mule) or an API gateway runtime instance. The instance can be running in CloudHub, in an
on-premises server of your organization, in a group of servers, or in a server cluster.

In the **Applications** menu, you can get a quick view of the applications that are currently
deployed and running for your account. Click an entry to display additional information (as shown
here), or click **Manage Application** to see the applicationâs [dashboard](monitoring-dashboards)
and settings page:

![AMC_ManageApplication](_images/amc-manageapplication.png)

After you create at least one application, you can see your applications listed here.

An application deployed to a server group runs on every server in the group.

## Servers Tab

This tab is used only to deploy apps to on-premises servers. CloudHub workers arenât displayed here.

In Runtime Manager, a "server" is a Mule or API gateway instance in a location other than CloudHub
(on-premises or in a different cloud server) that you must first register in the Runtime Manager
console. After you register and configure your server, you can deploy your applications to it from
the **Applications** tab.

![AMC_Servers](_images/amc-servers2.png)

From the **Servers** tab, select one of the following options to perform these tasks:

- Add a Server (registers the server with the Runtime Manager console)
- Start Mule or API gateway
- Create a server group
- Create a cluster
- Add and remove servers from a server group
- Add and remove servers from a cluster
- Stop an application on a server
- Delete a server, server group or cluster

For more about the options on this tab, see
[Servers, Server Groups, and Clusters](managing-servers).

## Switch Environments

If you have multiple environments in your organization, you can switch between them:

1.  From any screen in Runtime Manager, click the current environment, which is displayed in the
    upper left corner of the screen.
2.  Select the environment to switch to.
3.  Click **Switch**.

![managing deployed applications 0e10f](_images/managing-deployed-applications-0e10f.png)

## Start, Restart, Stop, or Delete an Application

1.  In **Applications**, click an application name to open its management panel on the right side of
    the screen.
2.  Next to the status, click the down-arrow (the menus differ according to the appâs current
    state):

    ![AMC_StopStartDeleteApp](_images/amc-restartapp.png)

3.  Click the action to perform:
    - **Start**

      Starts stopped applications

    - **Restart**

      Resets applications that have already started. If the application is undeployed, the
      applicationâs ZIP file is sent to the server to start the application on the server.

    - **Stop**

      Stops running applications.

    - **Delete**

      Deletes the application.

If youâre deploying your application to an on-premises cluster, Runtime Manager starts/stops/deletes
the application simultaneously on each server.

> [!IMPORTANT] On CloudHub, after you delete an application, your log data is no longer accessible
> from the console. CloudHub archives old log data for a limited period of time before it is purged.
> This allows you to recover the data if needed. Open a support case at:
> [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com) for more information.

## Status States

The information in the **Status** column depends on the context:

- [Application Status States](#application_status_states)
- [Application Deployment States](#application_deploy_states)
- [Replica Status States](#replica_status_states)
- [Server Status States](#server_status_states)
- [Server Groups Status States](#server_groups_status_states)
- [Cluster Status States](#cluster_status_states)

### Application Status States

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Icon</th><th>State</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-a665b.png" alt="managing deployed applications a665b"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Stopped</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Not Running</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Undeployed</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The application is not running.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-515a4.png" alt="managing deployed applications 515a4"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Starting</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Stopping</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Undeploying</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application is in the process of transitioning to another state.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/console-overview-green-circle.png" alt="console_overview_green_circle"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Started</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Running</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Updated</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application is running or the update succeeded.</p></div><div><p>The Application shows Started when running on at least one of the Cluster nodes.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/console-overview-red-circle.png" alt="console_overview_red_circle"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Deployment Failed</strong></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application deployment failed.</p></div><div><p>The cause of the failure appears in the console.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-ab70d.png" alt="managing deployed applications ab70d"></div></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application update failed.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Application Deployment States

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Icon</th><th>State</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-a665b.png" alt="managing deployed applications a665b"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Deleted</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application is deleted.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-515a4.png" alt="managing deployed applications 515a4"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Applying</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application is in the process of transitioning to another state.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/console-overview-green-circle.png" alt="console_overview_green_circle"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Applied</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application is running or the update succeeded.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/console-overview-red-circle.png" alt="console_overview_red_circle"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Failed</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Application deployment failed.</p></div><div><p>The cause of the failure appears in the console.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-de1b7.png" alt="managing deployed applications de1b7"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Unknown</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Applicationâs status is unknown because the server is not responding to the platform (on-premises deployments only).</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Replica Status States

Replica statuses apply only to applications deployed on Runtime Fabric. These statuses appear when
you are applying new, or changed, configurations to deployed applications. See
[Application Deployment Versioning](../runtime-fabric/latest/app-versioning).

Replica statuses include:

- **Started** (green)

  Replica is receiving traffic.

- **Pending**

  Replica is waiting to start.

- **Starting**

  Process is starting on the replica.

- **Deleting**

  Replica deletion is in progress.

- **Deleted**

  Replica has been deleted.

- **Stopping**

  Replica is stopping.

- **Stopped**

  Replica is stopped (not receiving traffic).

- **Failed** (red)

  Configuration failed to deploy.

- **Recovering** (red)

  Replica is attempting to restart after terminating.

### Server Status States

- **Connected**

  Server is available for use.

- **Created**

  Server was recently registered into the system and has never been connected.

- **Running**

  Server is up and running, and accepting requests.

- **Disconnected**

  Server is currently unavailable but is registered and ready to connect.

### Server Groups Status States

- **Connected**

  All servers in the server group are connected.

- **Created**

  Server group created.

  This state occurs temporarily after creating a group until Empty asserts.

- **Disconnected**

  All servers in the server group are unavailable. This state can occur if the network goes down or
  one or more servers in a group fail.

- **Empty**

  No servers are currently assigned to this group.

- **Partial**

  One or more servers in the group have differing states.

- **Running**

  All servers in the server group are running.

### Cluster Status States

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Icon</th><th>State</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/status-green.png" alt="green"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Running</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All nodes are running.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/status-yellow.png" alt="yellow"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Running</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>At least one of the Cluster nodes is running.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/status-yellow.png" alt="yellow"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Running with communication issue</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All nodes are running, but communication issues exist between them.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><div><img src="_images/managing-deployed-applications-a665b.png" alt="managing deployed applications a665b"></div></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Disconnected</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All nodes are disconnected.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><span><img src="_images/status-black.png" alt="black"></span></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Created</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A server/node is registered in the platform but is not started.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Applications Management Panel

If you select any of the applications on the **Applications** tab, a panel opens on the right. Here
you can see additional information about the application and access its settings.

![dashboard](_images/dashboard-ch.png)

What the management panel displays depends on whether your application is deployed on
[CloudHub](../cloudhub/managing-applications-on-cloudhub) or on
[your servers](managing-applications-on-your-own-servers):

- Applications on CloudHub display information about the CloudHub worker on which they run.
- Applications deployed on clusters and server groups display the list of servers and the status of
  each.

You always see the status, the time of the last modification, and the deployed application ZIP file.
The **Choose File** button enables you to change the application ZIP file.

The **Manage Application** button enables you to view and configure several more application
settings:

![managing deployed applications 38b94](_images/managing-deployed-applications-38b94.png)

In the appâs settings page, the **Dashboard** button displays the full detail dashboard for your
application. See [Monitoring Dashboards](monitoring-dashboards).

![managing deployed applications 107ad](_images/managing-deployed-applications-107ad.png)

> [!NOTE] The **Dashboard** option is available only if your organization has the Anypoint
> Integration Advanced package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform.

The **Insight** button enables you to see data at a transaction level. See [Insight](insight).

![managing deployed applications 76124](_images/managing-deployed-applications-76124.png)

> [!NOTE] The dashboard and insights features are not currently available for Anypoint Platform
> Private Cloud Edition. See [Deployment Options](deployment-strategies).

## Monitor Applications

The tools you use to monitor application events and server or virtual server performance depend on
the [deployment option](deployment-strategies). See [Monitor Applications](monitoring).

## Update an Application

If you made changes to your application and want to deploy a new version:

1.  Click **Choose file** on the deployment screen.
2.  Select either **Import file from Exchange** or **Upload file**.
3.  Click **Apply changes**.  
    Within a few seconds, your application redeploys. While redeploying, the application status
    indicator changes to blue, and then turns green after the deployment completes.
4.  For applications deployed to CloudHub, you can click **Logs** to see a live redeployment of your
    application.

If youâre deploying your application to an on-premises cluster, Runtime Manager installs the new app
version simultaneously on each server.

To update applications deployed to Runtime Fabric, see
[Application Versioning on Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/app-versioning).

### Grace Period

When you stop, restart, update, redeploy, or terminate an application, CloudHub tries to delete it
from Mule. To make sure inflight messages are delivered, thereâs a two-minute timeout period. After
that period, CloudHub shuts down the whole instance. This grace period applies to the application
instance, not to the runtime. The runtime uses its own configuration for shutdown. By default, the
runtime grace period is five seconds.

## Alerts

You can set up email alerts that are sent whenever certain events occur to your application, such as
deployment failures or application deletions. These alerts can be linked to a specific application
or to all applications. See [Alerts](alerts-on-runtime-manager) for information.

Even if you donât have permission to create alerts, you can specify whether to receive email alerts.

Switching an alert off from this view switches it off for the current (signed in) user only. Other
users can still have active alerts.

## See Also

- [Manage Applications on CloudHub](../cloudhub/managing-applications-on-cloudhub)
- [Servers, Server Groups, and Clusters](managing-servers)
- [Send Data from Runtime Manager to External Analytics Software](sending-data-from-arm-to-external-analytics-software)
- [Deployment Options](deployment-strategies)
- [Monitor Applications and Servers](monitoring)
- [Clustering](../cloudhub/cloudhub-fabric)
- [Manage Queues](../cloudhub/managing-queues)
- [Manage Schedules](../cloudhub/managing-schedules)
- [Manage Application Data Storage with Object Stores](../cloudhub/managing-application-data-with-object-stores)
- [Safely Hide Application Properties](../cloudhub/secure-application-properties)
- [Virtual Private Cloud](../cloudhub/virtual-private-cloud)
- [Penetration Testing Policies](../cloudhub/penetration-testing-policies)
- [Application Versioning on Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/app-versioning)
